gaps.what: The single biggest telemetry gap. B.2 demands "Every allocation and free is attributed to a resource kind, lifetime, role, and operation so aggregate wins cannot hide one catastrophically weak class." Today attribution is LITERALLY THROWN AWAY: res_account_copy (resources_registry.c:220) and res_account_transfer (resources_registry.c:231) both open with `(void)plan;`. Every counter lives in one flat global `ano_res_allocator_stats g_reg.stats` (resources_registry.c:107). owned_alloc_locked (resources_registry.c:154-182) receives the full res_place_plan and ignores every field except lifetime; block_free_locked (resources_registry.c:196-209) has plan available on res_owned_block (resources_internal.h:118) and ignores it too. COMPLETE = an SoA counter cube keyed by (res_kind[10] x ano_res_lifetime_kind[6] x res_role[7] x res_operation[9]) -- res_kind/res_role/res_operation/res_destination/res_provenance are already defined at resources_internal.h:39-95 -- charged on EVERY alloc, free, copy, transfer, promotion, duplication, retire; plus a public query API (e.g. ano_res_stats_by(kind,lifetime,role,op) or a bulk snapshot of the cube) so a contestant's weak class cannot hide in an aggregate. The cube must be allocated from the ROOT domain like every other registry metadata block (root_plan, resources_registry.c:331).
gaps.spec_ref: B.2 bullet 6 ("Every allocation and free is attributed to a resource kind, lifetime, role, and operation"); A.4 bullet 5

gaps.what: ano_res_allocator_stats (include/anoptic_resources.h:111-133) is missing over half the B.2 list. PRESENT: requested_bytes, serving_bytes, live_bytes/blocks, peak_bytes/blocks, chunk_bytes, chunk_count, allocations, frees, copies, bytes_copied, promotions, duplications, transfers, transfer_bytes, retired_pending, stalled_readers, descriptors_live, domains_live, rows_bound. MISSING ENTIRELY: parent calls, class hits, oversize hits, internal fragmentation, external fragmentation, promotion cost, release mode, retire cost, registry probes, cache/TLB counters, residual footprint after every cycle. Also broken as specified: requested_bytes/serving_bytes are CUMULATIVE (owned_alloc_locked resources_registry.c:174-176 only ever adds; block_free_locked resources_registry.c:205-207 decrements only live_bytes/live_blocks), so `serving - requested` is a lifetime total and NOT the live internal fragmentation B.2 wants. COMPLETE = add live_requested_bytes (so live internal frag = live_bytes - live_requested_bytes), parent_calls, class_hits[], oversize_hits, external_frag_bytes, promotion_bytes/ns, retire_ns, registry_probes, releases_zero_copy, releases_copied, and a per-domain residual accessor.
gaps.spec_ref: B.2 bullet 5 (the full per-model stats list)

gaps.what: ano_mem_stats (include/anoptic_memory_pools.h:81-88) carries only live_bytes, live_blocks, peak_bytes, peak_blocks, chunk_bytes, chunk_count. The registry can therefore never report B.2's allocator-level numbers, because it has no source for them. src/memory/pools.c: stats_on_alloc/stats_on_free (:35-47) count SERVING size only -- the request size is discarded, so internal fragmentation is unmeasurable at the pool. pacquire/prelease (parent calls) are uncounted. The multipool's per-class array `pool_core cls[]` (pools.c:397) has no per-class hit counter, so "class hits" does not exist. mp_oversize_alloc (pools.c:439) tracks oversize BYTES on the chunk counters but never counts an oversize HIT. Free-list residency per class is not exposed, so "external fragmentation" (chunk bytes held vs. live bytes served, per class) cannot be computed. COMPLETE = extend ano_mem_stats with requested_bytes (live), parent_acquires, parent_releases, oversize_hits, and a per-class {hits, live_blocks, free_blocks, stride} array accessor (a separate ano_mem_multipool_class_stats() keeps the common struct small); mirror the parent-call counters into ano_mem_pool and ano_mem_monotonic. ano_mem_pool still has NO production consumer, so it must be counted too before Model D's chunk pools can be judged.
gaps.spec_ref: B.2 bullet 5 (parent calls, class hits, oversize hits, internal/external fragmentation)

gaps.what: B.2 requires "registry probes". probe_find (resources_registry.c:284-295) walks the open-addressed slot table in a loop with zero instrumentation; slot_insert (resources_registry.c:297-303) does the same. grow_slots (resources_registry.c:308-329) rehashes every bound row with no rehash counter. COMPLETE = count probes on lookup and on insert (total probes, max probe distance, rehashes, load factor at rehash), charged into the stats cube under RES_ROLE_REGISTRY / RES_OP_BIND, and expose them so a contestant whose registry shard lives in a hostile allocator shows the cache cost.
gaps.spec_ref: B.2 bullet 5 ("registry probes")

gaps.what: A.4 bullet 5 requires every LOAD and PARSE to be visible. Today the entire staging tier is invisible to ano_res_stats. (1) res_read_all (src/resources/resources_core.c:513-569) allocates the read buffer from the DEFAULT heap -- mi_malloc_aligned at :522, mi_realloc_aligned growth at :537, tightening realloc at :562 -- with no counter; ano_res_get calls it at resources_registry.c:787 and accounts only the copy INTO the home (res_account_copy at :806). No bytes_read, no read ops, no growth-realloc count, no short-read count. (2) res_graphics.c ingest stands up a whole private mi_heap + monotonic arena (src/resources/graphics/res_graphics.c:359-365) for cgltf, and gfx_slurp (res_graphics.c:110-125) reads every external .bin/.png URI into it -- none of it appears in any statistic. (3) save_probe_file (resources_registry.c:1220) reads every save frame through res_read_all into the default heap, uncounted. COMPLETE = a staging accounting seam (res_account_staging(plan, bytes) or a res_place_plan with RES_ROLE_STAGING / RES_DEST_STAGING -- both enums already exist, resources_internal.h:58/:78, and have NO current user) charged from res_read_all, gfx_slurp, and the cgltf arena, plus an IO counter block (bytes_read, files_opened, candidate probes across res_candidates at resources_core.c:503, short reads, growth reallocs) needed for the remote-FS floor evidence.
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 5 ("every load, parse, condition, copy... visible"); B.2 bullet 5

gaps.what: B.2 requires "release mode" as a reported metric. ano_res_release (resources_registry.c:843-899) bumps `transfers` and `transfer_bytes` in BOTH branches -- the zero-copy pointer move (:870-871) and the pooled copy-out (:886-888) -- so a reader of ano_res_stats cannot tell how many releases were actually zero-copy. The copy branch additionally bumps copies/bytes_copied/duplications (:884-887), so it is inferable by subtraction only if nothing else in the process ever copies, which is false. The ONLY real proof of zero-copy today is the pointer-equality oracle inside the disabled bench (tests/anotest_resbench.c:362-363). COMPLETE = explicit releases_zero_copy / releases_copied / release_copy_bytes counters attributed by kind+lifetime, and a pointer-equality oracle promoted out of the disabled bench into an enabled test. Also note res_account_transfer (declared resources_internal.h:127, defined resources_registry.c:231) has ZERO callers -- ano_res_release inlines its own accounting instead. It is a dead seam and must either be wired up or deleted.
gaps.spec_ref: B.2 bullet 5 ("copies and bytes copied, promotion cost, release mode, retire cost")

gaps.what: res_registry_external_allocation (resources_registry.c:242-253) adds requested_bytes, serving_bytes, allocations, transfers, transfer_bytes -- but touches NEITHER live_bytes NOR live_blocks, and there is no matching res_registry_external_free anywhere. Its only caller is ano_resgfx_image (src/resources/graphics/res_graphics.c:683), which hands the raw stbi block to the renderer; the renderer frees it with ano_aligned_free (src/render/gltf/ano_GltfParser.c:200) with zero accounting. Decoded-image residency is therefore permanently unmeasurable and the `allocations` counter is unbalanced against `frees` by exactly the number of decoded textures. COMPLETE = res_registry_external_free(plan, bytes) called by the consumer (or a manager-owned decoded-image placement so the block never leaves the domain -- the contest is supposed to decide this, per A.2 "Fonts and images" row), plus live/peak accounting for external blocks so alloc/free balance.
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 5; B.2 bullet 5 (live/peak bytes and blocks, residual footprint); A.2 "Fonts and images" row

gaps.what: parseGltf copies the ENTIRE conditioned scene out of manager memory into plain calloc'd legacy blueprints and then unloads the handle -- a copy + hand-off + retirement chain that A.4 bullet 5 says must be visible, and none of it is. Sites: calloc(ModelAsset) at src/render/gltf/ano_GltfParser.c:120, calloc(ModelMesh) at :126, calloc(ModelPrimitive) at :131, the four per-image arrays at :154-157, the staging-buffer array at :180, and ano_res_unload of the scene at :423. These allocations belong to no domain and appear in no counter. COMPLETE = either (preferred, and what B.2 "Each model runs with real resource scopes in the engine" demands) the renderer retains manager views/handles instead of copying, or the copy is charged through res_account_copy with a RES_ROLE_TRANSFER / RES_OP_TRANSFER plan and the blueprint blocks are allocated from a real lifetime domain.
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 5; A.2 "Graphics extension" row ("consumers do not yet retain uniform manager views/handles"); B.2 last bullet

gaps.what: B.2 requires "residual footprint after every cycle", and B.5/B.7 judge the models on exactly this. ano_res_stats (resources_registry.c:990-1007) folds chunk_bytes/chunk_count across EVERY live domain into one global number by looping the domains and summing ano_mem_multipool_stats. res_domain (resources_registry.c:65-73) already tracks heap, pool, kind, generation, state, live_rows, pending_blocks -- none of it is exposed. Direct mi_heap blocks (>1 MiB, owned_alloc_locked resources_registry.c:163-165) contribute NOTHING to chunk_bytes, so the domain's true footprint is not even computable from the pool stats. anotest_resbench.c:312-324 reconstructs a "residual" by subtracting global snapshots, which cannot attribute residue to a domain and silently ignores the direct-block tier. COMPLETE = ano_res_domain_stats(ano_res_lifetime, ano_res_allocator_stats *out) reporting that domain's own pool chunk bytes + direct-block bytes + registry-shard bytes + live rows + pending blocks, so a cycle test can assert "domain retired -> residual == 0" per domain rather than by global subtraction.
gaps.spec_ref: B.2 bullet 5 ("residual footprint after every cycle"); B.5/B.7 home ground

gaps.what: "The contest cannot judge work it cannot see" -- and today nothing is machine-readable. tests/templates/bench.h gives bench_lat_stats (p50/p95/p99/p999/max) and bench_ops_per_sec, all printf'd to stdout only (bench_lat_row, bench.h:103). docs/benchmarks/ contains only hand-written .md files -- no run has ever deposited raw samples there, which B.9 requires verbatim ("Preserve raw machine-readable samples under docs/benchmarks/"). Worse, the bench's own stats shim is a lie: res_registry_stats() (tests/anotest_resbench.c:42-51) fabricates an ano_mem_stats from registry fields and then sets direct_bytes = s.live_bytes and direct_blocks = s.live_blocks -- the SAME numbers it just put in pools.live_bytes/live_blocks. The "chunks +N, direct +N" split printed at anotest_resbench.c:320-324 and the assertions at :335-337 are therefore comparing a number against itself; the pooled-vs-direct split it claims to report does not exist. anotest_resbench is also DISABLED in ctest. COMPLETE = a bench_report writer emitting JSON/CSV samples into docs/benchmarks/<stamp>-<scenario>.json (raw per-op ticks + the full stats cube snapshot before/after each phase), res_registry_stats() rewritten against the real per-domain/per-class accessors, and the bench enabled behind an opt-in that actually runs in the matrix.
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 5 ("benchmark counters"); B.9 ("Preserve raw machine-readable samples under docs/benchmarks/")

gaps.what: ano_res_stats() (resources_registry.c:990) returns one by-value global snapshot and there is no way to bracket a phase: no reset, no delta, no per-lifetime or per-kind filter. Every existing consumer hand-rolls a subtraction (tests/anotest_resownership.c:139/163/172-174, tests/anotest_resgroups.c:87/117, tests/anotest_resbench.c:44). With cumulative-vs-live fields mixed in one struct (see tel-stats-missing-fields) those subtractions are subtly wrong for serving_bytes/requested_bytes. COMPLETE = a stats snapshot/delta helper (ano_res_stats_delta(before, after)) plus filtered queries over the attribution cube, so "the contest" can bracket ingest/condition/retire phases per kind and lifetime without every caller reinventing it.
gaps.spec_ref: B.2 bullet 5; B.9 (per-scenario measurement discipline)

gaps.what: A.4 bullet 6 names this one explicitly: "including legacy path-based font loading". The path-based route is STILL FULLY ALIVE. Declaration: include/anoptic_text.h:39 (`AnoFontId ano_text_font_load(anostr_t path)`) plus the convenience macro at include/anoptic_text.h:42 (`ano_text_font_load_lit`). Implementation: src/text/text.c:129-179, with the actual filesystem open at src/text/text.c:156 (`FT_New_Face(g_ftLibrary, cpath, 0, &face)`) and its error log at src/text/text.c:160. GOOD NEWS: there is NO production caller -- src/vulkan_backend/text_raster.c uses ano_text_font_load_memory exclusively (text_raster.c:588, :603, :608) over ano_res_bytes. The ONLY callers are tests: tests/anotest_text.c:895, :897, :898, :900, using the literals FONT_PATH (tests/anotest_text.c:31) and RUNE_FONT_PATH (tests/anotest_text.c:32). COMPLETE = delete the decl (anoptic_text.h:39), the macro (:42), and the impl (text.c:129-179); FT_New_Face then appears nowhere in src/ (only in external/freetype). Rewrite the anotest_text.c font block to ano_res_init + mount + ano_res_get + a held ano_res_read scope + ano_text_font_load_memory -- the read scope MUST outlive the face because FreeType borrows the blob (documented at anoptic_text.h:44-47), which is a real structural change to that test, not a find/replace.
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 6; A.2 "Fonts and images" row ("the public path-based ano_text_font_load/FT_New_Face route remains")

gaps.what: ano_fs_chdir_gamepath is the CWD-mutating shim every older plan scheduled for deletion; the report confirms it survived (docs/resourcemanager-comprehensive-report.md:84). Declaration: include/anoptic_filesystem.h:64. Implementations in ALL THREE platform TUs: src/filesystem/filesystem_win64.c:67, src/filesystem/filesystem_linux.c:71, src/filesystem/filesystem_macos.c:76. Zero production callers (main.c's shim is gone -- src/engine/main.c:627 says so). THE BLAST RADIUS IS THE TEST SUITE: tests/templates/scratch.h:32 wraps it as `scratch_anchor_to_exe()`, and scratch.h's entire relative-path model depends on the chdir (scratch.h:20: "." means "the CWD"). scratch_anchor_to_exe callers: tests/anotest_blackbox.c:744, tests/anotest_logtail.c:101, tests/anotest_logstrbench.c:160, tests/anotest_logfuzz.c:168, tests/anotest_logbench.c:277, tests/anotest_resownership.c:240, tests/anotest_resources.c:746, tests/anotest_resgroups.c:61, tests/anotest_resgfx.c:333, tests/anotest_resfault.c:78, tests/anotest_resdurability.c:103, tests/anotest_resbench.c:416, tests/anotest_persistence.c:378. Direct callers: tests/anotest_logging.c:1116 and tests/anotest_filesystem.c:45 (which ASSERTS the function succeeds). COMPLETE = rebase tests/templates/scratch.h on ano_fs_gamepath() + absolute joins (no process-wide chdir), delete the assertion at anotest_filesystem.c:45 and the call at anotest_logging.c:1116, then delete the decl and all three impls. Doing the deletion before the scratch.h rebase breaks 15 test binaries.
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 6 ("Remove every alternate asset IO ... path outside resource extensions"); report line 84

gaps.what: Two unowned escape hatches survive INSIDE the public resource API and are the mechanism by which any future code can reopen a file by path. (1) ano_res_slurp (include/anoptic_resources.h:146, impl src/resources/resources_core.c:571) reads into a caller-supplied mi_heap, bypassing the registry, every generation guard, and every counter. Zero production callers; used only by tests (tests/anotest_resources.c:88, :124, :137, :143-144, :261, :270, :276, :288, :296; tests/anotest_resdurability.c:96). (2) The block the header itself labels "Resolution migration debt" (include/anoptic_resources.h:137-142): ano_res_resolve, ano_res_resolve_write, ano_res_subpath, ano_res_exists -- these hand a raw ano_fspath to ANY caller. Production uses none of them except resources_core.c:680 internally; tests use them heavily (tests/anotest_resdurability.c:133, tests/anotest_persistence.c:79, tests/anotest_resources.c:117-318). A third, module-internal one: gfx_slurp (src/resources/graphics/res_graphics.c:110-125) reads glTF .bin/.png URIs straight through res_read_all into a scratch heap -- legal under A.4 (it IS the resource extension) but invisible to accounting, see tel-staging-invisible. COMPLETE = delete ano_res_slurp outright (rewrite its test users onto get/bytes inside a read scope), and either delete the four resolve/subpath/exists entry points or move them behind an explicitly-named test/tool-only internal header so no engine TU can reach a raw path.
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 6 ("tools and importers also enter through the resource namespace and extension grammar")

gaps.what: The demo scene is still owned by renderer code as string literals: src/vulkan_backend/render_api.c:143 ("models/viking_room.gltf"), :147 ("models/GlassHurricaneCandleHolder.gltf"), :152 ("models/sponza/2.0/Sponza/glTF/Sponza.gltf"), with the spawn logic keyed to those indices at src/engine/main.c:131-135 ("Sponza (asset_id 2)"). Font logicals are hardcoded at src/vulkan_backend/text_raster.c:28, :29, :30. Roughly 30 shader logicals are hardcoded across src/vulkan_backend/instance/pipeline.c:64, pipelines/compute.c:80/:146/:188/:243/:250/:311/:369/:424/:457, pipelines/flat.c:71/:76/:83-86, pipelines/shadow_pipe.c:48/:52/:158/:162/:206/:210, pipelines/transmission.c:64/:71/:72, pipelines/additive.c:60/:65, pipelines/tonemap.c:52/:54, text_raster.c:342/:370/:373/:474/:477. IMPORTANT DISTINCTION: the shader and font literals are LOGICAL NAMES that already route through ano_res_get (inside ano_pipeline_shader, src/vulkan_backend/instance/pipeline.c:24-57) -- they are not alternate loaders, and the only gap there is SID/interned naming. The MODEL literals are different: they are the renderer owning the demo scene's asset list, which A.2 ("Other resource domains" row) requires eliminating in favour of a data-driven level. COMPLETE = the world/level domain declares the scene's models; ano_render_load_scene_assets consumes that declaration instead of the three literals; the asset_id 0/1/2 coupling in main.c:131-135 dies with it.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Other resource domains" row ("Eliminate hardcoded demo asset ownership from renderer code"); A.4 bullet 6

gaps.what: A.4 bullet 6 ends "tools and importers also enter through the resource namespace and extension grammar." Two classes of surviving direct file IO must be RULED ON IN WRITING rather than swept, or a deletion pass will destroy the test suite. (a) tools/gen_unicode_tables.c:82 and :422 open tools/ucd/ with fopen. It is an offline BUILD-TIME codegen tool, never linked into the engine, and reads no game asset -- the defensible call is an explicit written exemption ("not an asset importer"), not a rewrite. (b) The test oracles: tests/anotest_resbench.c:126 (write_file), tests/anotest_resources.c:70/:79/:386/:503/:516/:518, tests/anotest_resgfx.c:98/:288, tests/anotest_resgroups.c:32, tests/anotest_resownership.c:45, tests/anotest_resfault.c:47, tests/templates/scratch.h:48, tests/anotest_logging.c:81, tests/anotest_blackbox.c:401/:719/:732 all use raw fopen. These are the INDEPENDENT ORACLES -- routing them through the manager destroys their power to falsify it. COMPLETE = a short written policy in docs/ naming both exemptions and the reason, so the deletion gate ("grep proves no alternate path remains", C.12 step 5) has a defined allow-list to grep against.
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 6; C.12 step 5 ("only after grep ... prove no alternate path remains")

integration_points: src/resources/resources_registry.c:107 -- `static struct { ... ano_res_allocator_stats stats; } g_reg;` The single flat counter block. EVERY telemetry change touches this struct, and the lock-free/owner-thread squad is rewriting the same struct's mtx/rows/domains. Coordinate: telemetry adds fields, it must not reorder or relocate the existing ones.

src/resources/resources_registry.c:154 owned_alloc_locked() and :196 block_free_locked() -- the two chokepoints where every owned byte is born and dies. Attribution must be charged HERE, not at the call sites. Any squad adding a new placement strategy (the A-E contest) will rewrite these two functions; the counter cube must be updated inside them so a new contestant cannot silently skip accounting.

src/resources/resources_registry.c:220 res_account_copy / :231 res_account_transfer / :242 res_registry_external_allocation -- the three public accounting seams in resources_internal.h:126-137. res_account_transfer currently has ZERO callers. res_graphics.c:683 is the only caller of res_registry_external_allocation. Signatures will change (they must actually USE the plan); every caller listed in the ledger must be updated in the same commit.

src/resources/resources_internal.h:97-106 res_place_plan -- the neutral placement vocabulary. It is ALREADY complete enough to key the attribution cube (kind/lifetime/role/operation/destination/provenance). The allocator-contest squad will extend it. Telemetry must key off the existing five enums and NOT add a sixth axis without coordinating, or the cube explodes.

include/anoptic_memory_pools.h:81-88 ano_mem_stats -- shared by monotonic, multipool, and pool. Adding fields changes the ABI for tests/anotest_mempools.c (which reads .live_bytes/.chunk_bytes at :87-356) and tests/anotest_resbench.c:37. The memory squad and the telemetry squad collide here; ano_mem_stats should GROW, never shrink or reorder.

src/memory/pools.c:35 stats_on_alloc / :43 stats_on_free / :400 mp_class_of / :397 pool_core cls[] -- the multipool hot path. Per-class hit counters land in pool_core; this is on the allocation fast path, so the counters must be plain (non-atomic) stores consistent with the single-owner contract stated in anoptic_memory_pools.h:31-34.

include/anoptic_resources.h:111-135 ano_res_allocator_stats + ano_res_stats() -- the public telemetry surface. Consumers: tests/anotest_resownership.c:139/163/172-174, tests/anotest_resgroups.c:87/92/117, tests/anotest_resbench.c:44. Growing the struct is source-compatible; renaming or repurposing requested_bytes/serving_bytes (which are cumulative today, and arguably should not be) is NOT.

src/resources/resources_core.c:513 res_read_all() -- the one gulp primitive. Called by ano_res_get (resources_registry.c:787), ano_res_slurp (resources_core.c:588), gfx_slurp (res_graphics.c:118), and save_probe_file (resources_registry.c:1220). The ranged-read / chunk-pool / codec squad will rewrite this function; IO telemetry must be added to it, and both squads must agree on the signature (it currently takes a raw mi_heap_t*, which is exactly the un-planned allocation A.4 bullet 5 forbids).

tests/templates/scratch.h:32 scratch_anchor_to_exe() -- the ano_fs_chdir_gamepath wrapper that 13 test binaries call in main(). Deleting the chdir requires rewriting this header first. EVERY squad that touches a test's main() will hit this file.

src/vulkan_backend/instance/pipeline.c:24 ano_pipeline_shader() -- registers AND unregisters an ano_res_reader per call. RES_READER_MAX is 64 (resources_registry.c:26). Same pattern at src/render/gltf/ano_GltfParser.c:93. The read-scope/reclamation squad owns this lane budget; telemetry wants a stalled_readers/high-water counter on it (stalled_readers exists at resources_registry.c:476 but only accumulates, it is not a high-water mark).
risks: The attribution cube is a 10x6x7x9 = 3780-cell counter space. Naive `size_t counters[10][6][7][9][N_METRICS]` is megabytes and will wreck the cache on the alloc path. It must be an SoA design with a compact packed key and only the metrics B.2 actually names, or it becomes the very cache pathology the contest is trying to measure. Sizing must be justified before it is written.

Counters on owned_alloc_locked/block_free_locked sit INSIDE the g_reg mutex today. Stage C deletes that mutex (C.13 deletion gate: "no g_reg.mtx ... remains"). If telemetry writes plain non-atomic counters now, they become a data race the moment the owner thread is introduced. Design the counters as owner-thread-only from the start (the owner_thread() gate at resources_registry.c:110 already exists) and make ano_res_stats() publish an immutable snapshot rather than read the live struct under a lock.

res_account_copy / res_account_transfer / res_registry_external_allocation do NOT check owner_thread() -- they only check g_reg.alive (resources_registry.c:222, :233, :244). They are callable from any thread today and are protected only by the mutex. Adding attribution without fixing this bakes in a cross-thread write to the counter cube.

requested_bytes and serving_bytes are cumulative while live_bytes is current, in the same struct, with no naming that says so (include/anoptic_resources.h:112-114). Existing tests already subtract snapshots of them. Any "fix" that makes them live will silently change the meaning of tests/anotest_resownership.c:172-174 and tests/anotest_resgroups.c:87. Add new live_* fields rather than redefining the old ones.

Deleting ano_fs_chdir_gamepath is a 15-file test change and touches the log/blackbox tests, which are NOT this effort's area. The scratch.h rebase must land as its own commit, green, before the filesystem TUs are touched -- otherwise a resource-manager squad reddens the logger suite and the failure looks like a resource bug.

tests/anotest_text.c is the only consumer of ano_text_font_load. Converting it to ano_text_font_load_memory forces the text test to stand up the whole resource manager (init + mount + reader + read scope) and to HOLD that read scope for the lifetime of every FT_Face, because FreeType borrows the blob (anoptic_text.h:44-47). If the read scope is closed early the face dereferences retired memory and the failure will present as a FreeType crash, not a resource-manager error. ASan/TSan runs of anotest_text are mandatory after that change.

anotest_resbench's res_registry_stats() (tests/anotest_resbench.c:42-51) sets direct_bytes = live_bytes and pools.live_bytes = live_bytes -- the same value twice. Its "(b) cycle N: residual footprint ... (chunks +N, direct +N)" report (:320-324) and its two closing assertions (:335-337) are therefore self-comparisons. Any recorded number from this bench that claims a pooled-vs-direct split is not evidence and must not be carried forward into the contest tables.

res_registry_external_allocation never balances against a free, so `allocations` already exceeds `frees` by the count of every decoded texture ever produced. Any test that asserts allocations == frees at shutdown will fail the moment a texture is decoded -- which is why no such test exists. Adding one is the correct oracle, but it will go red immediately and must land WITH the external-free fix, not before it.

The shader/font logical literals (~30 sites) look like hardcoded asset paths to a grep-driven deletion sweep, but they already route through ano_res_get. Deleting or rewriting them buys nothing and risks breaking pipeline creation. The real gap there is SID/interned naming, which is a different (and much lower-value) item than the model literals in render_api.c:143-152. Do not conflate them.

gaps.what: CORRECTNESS BUG, blocks the whole area. `ano_res_get` (src/resources/resources_registry.c:756) validates the requested lifetime is live (:765), binds the row (:769), then at :775-779 returns the EXISTING handle whenever `row->payload.data != NULL` WITHOUT comparing `row->payload.plan.lifetime` to the requested `lifetime`. The registry is keyed by rid globally and there is exactly one row per logical path, so a resource already resident in domain A is silently served to a caller who asked for domain B. Consequences today are latent only because production loads everything into the immortal ENGINE domain; the moment two WORLD_LEVEL domains exist, level B holds handles into level A's heap, `ano_res_unload(B, h)` refuses with -1 (lifetime mismatch, :827-830), and `ano_res_domain_retire(A)` invalidates B's live handles. This is exactly the 'silent cross-lifetime sharing' A.4 bullet 7 forbids — worse than a refcount, because nothing is counted. COMPLETE: the hit path compares the resident owner to the requested lifetime and takes an explicit policy branch — same domain => RETAIN (return handle); different domain => consult `res_disposition_allowed` and either refuse (sentinel + one ERROR line), serve a SHARED_IMMUTABLE alias, or DUPLICATE into the requester's domain. No path may return a handle whose payload lives in a domain the caller did not name and cannot retire.
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 7 ("Resolve cross-lifetime sharing without silently adding reference counts"); A.2 "Lifetime hierarchy"

gaps.what: There is no shared-immutable domain. `ano_res_lifetime_kind` (include/anoptic_resources.h:40-47) has ENGINE, WORLD_LEVEL, STREAMING, TRANSIENT_IMPORT, SAVE_CONFIG, TOOL_IMPORT and nothing else, so the only answer to 'two levels want the same 4 MiB texture' is 'the first one owns it'. COMPLETE: add ANO_RES_LIFETIME_SHARED_IMMUTABLE **after** TOOL_IMPORT (the open-time bound check at resources_registry.c:601-602 is `kind > ANO_RES_LIFETIME_TOOL_IMPORT`, so inserting anywhere else silently breaks domain_open and must be updated in lockstep); give it a domain that never retires while any referencing domain is live; make it a legal PROMOTE target in `res_disposition_allowed` (which today permits promotion only to ENGINE, or TRANSIENT->TOOL, at :263-271); and have the level loader classify a declared asset as level-owned vs shared at disclosure time.
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 7; A.2 "Lifetime hierarchy" ("resources must enter them by explicit token"); B.5/B.7 shared-immutable domains

gaps.what: `res_disposition_allowed` (src/resources/resources_registry.c:255-282) is a PREDICATE ONLY: it returns 0/-1 and bumps `stats.promotions` / `stats.duplications` counters. It moves no bytes, rebinds no row, republishes no descriptor. It is called from exactly one place in the tree — tests/anotest_resownership.c:185-195. There is no `ano_res_promote`, no `ano_res_duplicate`, no share operation, anywhere. So the promotion/duplication counters in `ano_res_allocator_stats` currently count decisions nobody acts on. COMPLETE: implement the three named policies as real owner-thread operations. PROMOTE(from,to,h): allocate in `to`'s domain via `owned_alloc_locked`, copy (or pointer-move when `plan.transfer_compatible && !pooled` and both sides are standalone mi blocks — state which, count it), rewrite `row->payload.plan.lifetime`, decrement from->live_rows / increment to->live_rows, republish a fresh `res_pub` with a bumped generation, retire the old descriptor through the epoch queue. DUPLICATE(from,to,h,out): the registry is one-row-per-rid, so a duplicate needs its own logical key (e.g. "<logical>@<owner>") or the row must gain per-domain payload slots — this is a schema decision that must be made before anyone writes code against it. SHARE: bind the row into the shared-immutable domain and hand out aliasing handles that no level domain may unload. Every path charges `copies`/`bytes_copied`/`promotions`/`duplications`/`transfers` (A.4 bullet 5: no invisible copy).
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 7 ("Promotion, duplication, shared immutable domains, or another explicit policy must be implemented"); B.2 ("explicit cross-lifetime policy")

gaps.what: Dependency disclosure is WRITE-ONLY. `res_dependency_meta {rid, kind, flags}` (src/resources/resources_internal.h:108-112) is produced exactly once — src/resources/graphics/res_graphics.c:572-591 builds the glTF scene's dep list (source .gltf rid with flags=1, plus one entry per referenced image rid with flags=srgb) — stored into `row->dependency_storage` / `row->dependency_count` (resources_registry.c:964-975), counted into `res_pub.dependency_count` (:505), and freed on retire (:534-537). NOTHING EVER READS IT BACK. There is no accessor in resources_internal.h, none in anoptic_resources.h, and grep finds no consumer. `res_pub.kind` (:42) is likewise stored and never read. COMPLETE: `int res_registry_dependencies(const ano_res_read *read, anores_t h, res_dependency_meta *out, size_t cap)` (module-internal) returning the disclosed set for a live handle, plus a public counted accessor if a consumer outside src/resources/ needs it; and a `res_registry_kind(const ano_res_read *, anores_t)` so a typed view can refuse a wrong-kind handle. This is the prerequisite for prefetch — without readback, the dependency graph the graphics extension already builds is dead weight.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Other resource domains" ("resource-declared dependencies/prefetch"); A.4 bullet 3 ("dependency disclosure")

gaps.what: No prefetch entry point exists at any layer. The renderer discovers a glTF's textures only by walking the conditioned scene view and calling `ano_res_get` per image inside the upload loop (src/render/gltf/ano_GltfParser.c:183-189) — i.e. dependency resolution is an accident of the consumer's traversal order, not a manager service. COMPLETE: an owner-thread `ano_res_prefetch(ano_res_lifetime, const char *logical)` (Stage A: synchronous, returns count loaded / count failed) that loads the named resource, reads back its disclosed dependency set (see deps-readback), and transitively resolves the closure into the SAME lifetime domain (or the shared/promoted domain the policy names), with cycle refusal, a bounded depth, and per-edge accounting. Stage C later moves this onto tickets; the synchronous reference must exist first (A.4 bullet 4).
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Other resource domains" ("resource-declared dependencies/prefetch"); A.4 bullet 3

gaps.what: THERE IS NO LEVEL FORMAT. src/resources/world/res_world.c (188 lines) is a game-SAVE codec only — a 48-byte ANOW v2 blob holding simulation_tick, world_seed, camera pose and flags (include/anoptic_res_world.h:17-24), with v1->v2 migration. Nothing in the tree declares what a level CONTAINS. COMPLETE: a data-driven level resource. Recommended shape (schema "anoptic.level", version 1, jsmn like config/keybindings so no new dep): {schema, version, name, lifetime: "world_level", assets: [{logical, kind: model|image|font|shader|audio|script, required: bool, disposition: "level"|"promote_engine"|"shared", band: "now"|"later"}], instances: [{asset, transform (mat4 or TRS), motion {type, speed}}], lights: [{type, color, intensity, range, cone, transform, casts_shadow}], entry: {camera_position, camera_yaw, camera_pitch, world_seed}, scripts: [{logical, hook: "on_load"}], audio: [{logical, hook: "ambient"}]}. The `assets` array IS the disclosed dependency set: `res_registry_adopt` for the conditioned level block passes one `res_dependency_meta` per declared asset (rid = fnv1a64 of the logical path, kind = the declared kind, flags = disposition|band), so opening a level and prefetching its closure is one uniform mechanism shared with the glTF scene. Conditioned level block is offset-shaped like `scene_hdr` (res_graphics.c:421-448) so it can be baked later. Ship `assets/levels/demo.anolevel.json` reproducing today's hardcoded scene, and stage it in CMakeLists.txt beside assets/ (line ~290).
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Other resource domains" ("Land real data-driven level/world ... resource domains"); §2 ("levels are data-driven")

gaps.what: No production code ever opens a WORLD_LEVEL domain. `ano_res_domain_open(ANO_RES_LIFETIME_WORLD_LEVEL, ...)` appears ONLY in tests (tests/anotest_resgroups.c:69,95; anotest_resownership.c:183; anotest_resbench.c:67). src/engine/main.c:647-652 opens exactly one domain, SAVE_CONFIG, and every asset load — models (src/render/gltf/ano_GltfParser.c:90), shaders (src/vulkan_backend/instance/pipeline.c:31), fonts (src/vulkan_backend/text_raster.c:586-609) — rides `ano_res_lifetime_engine()`, the immortal domain. A.2 is explicit that this is the gap: "every production load uses immortal group 0". COMPLETE: `ano_reslevel_open(const char *logical, ano_res_lifetime *out_domain, anores_t *out_level)` — opens a WORLD_LEVEL domain, loads+conditions the level block into it, prefetches the disclosed closure into that domain (honoring per-asset disposition: level-owned / promote-to-engine / shared-immutable), returns the domain token and the level handle. `ano_reslevel_close(ano_res_lifetime domain)` — retires it via `ano_res_domain_retire`, which already invalidates publication, crosses the reader grace point and calls `mi_heap_destroy` (resources_registry.c:434-448). NOTE the hard constraint: `ano_res_get`, `ano_res_domain_open` and `res_registry_adopt` are all owner-thread-gated (`owner_thread()`, :110-113), so Stage A level loading is synchronous on the init thread — every squad's ingest path (audio, script, level) must obey this.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Lifetime hierarchy" ("Real level/world/streaming consumers must create explicit lifetime domains")

gaps.what: Demo assets are hardcoded string literals in renderer startup and the scene graph is hardcoded in the logic thread. src/vulkan_backend/render_api.c:143-156: `parseGltf(&ctx, "models/viking_room.gltf")`, `"models/GlassHurricaneCandleHolder.gltf"`, `"models/sponza/2.0/Sponza/glTF/Sponza.gltf"` into a positional `g_assets[0..2]` namespace. src/engine/main.c:117-184 `spawn_scene()` hardcodes each asset's root transform, motion type/speed, six scene lights and five candle lights. `parseGltf` (src/render/gltf/ano_GltfParser.c:86-90) takes a `const char *logical` and pins `ano_res_lifetime_engine()` internally. A.2 requires: "Eliminate hardcoded demo asset ownership from renderer code." COMPLETE: the level's `assets`/`instances`/`lights` arrays drive scene composition; `parseGltf` is re-signatured to take the level's lifetime token and an already-resident `anores_t` scene handle (the level prefetch loaded it), not a path — the renderer stops naming assets at all; `anoRenderAssetPrimitives`/`anoRenderAssetCount` (include/anoptic_render.h:84-90) are fed from the level view; main.c's `spawn_scene` becomes a walk over the level's instance/light arrays. The `register_default_material` TODO at render_api.c:118-121 ("this is fucked up ... Redo with the asset manager") is in scope here.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Other resource domains" ("Eliminate hardcoded demo asset ownership from renderer code"); §2

gaps.what: Decoded pixels are the last caller-owned asset class and are NOT registry resources. `ano_resgfx_image` (src/resources/graphics/res_graphics.c:651-690) decodes with stb into a `RES_KIND_IMAGE_DECODED` plan, then calls `res_registry_external_allocation` (:683) — which only ACCOUNTS the bytes into stats (resources_registry.c:242-253) — and hands the raw stbi buffer back for the renderer to `ano_aligned_free` (ano_GltfParser.c:200). It has no handle, no generation, no domain, no dependency edge to its source, and cannot be shared, promoted, hot-reloaded or retired by domain. Meanwhile the ONLY genuine derived resource in the tree is the conditioned glTF scene keyed "<source>#gfx" (res_graphics.c:347-348, RES_ROLE_DERIVED). COMPLETE: `anores_t ano_resgfx_image_decoded(ano_res_lifetime, const ano_res_read *, anores_t src)` adopting RGBA8 into the registry under "<source>#rgba" with RES_ROLE_DERIVED / RES_PROVENANCE_DECODED and the source rid as a disclosed dependency; renderer consumes a counted view inside a read scope, or takes an explicit destructive `ano_res_release` transfer (which requires the block be transfer-compatible — today only >1 MiB non-pooled blocks move zero-copy, resources_registry.c:865-890, everything else COPIES). Keep the caller-owned entry point only as an explicitly quarantined tool path, or delete it.
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 3 ("typed derived resources"); A.2 "Fonts and images" ("decoded pixels are caller-owned mimalloc blocks" -> must go behind the extension)

gaps.what: The typed-kind vocabulary is half-dead. `res_kind` (src/resources/resources_internal.h:39-50) declares RES_KIND_CONFIG and RES_KIND_WORLD; grep proves NOTHING assigns either. Kind is inferred purely from file extension by `kind_from_path` (resources_registry.c:744-754), which knows only .spv/.vert/.frag, .ttf/.otf, .png/.jpg/.jpeg and defaults everything else to RES_KIND_BYTES — so config/settings.json, config/keybindings.json and any level file load as anonymous BYTES. `res_pub.kind` (:42) is published and never read, so `ano_resgfx_scene` can be handed a font handle and only its own SCENE_MAGIC check (res_graphics.c:611) saves it. COMPLETE: kind is set at ingest by the owning extension (config/keybindings/world/level/audio/script each declare their kind in the `res_place_plan` they adopt with), `kind_from_path` learns the new extensions (.json under config/ -> CONFIG, .anolevel -> WORLD, .wav -> AUDIO_ENCODED, .anos -> SCRIPT_SOURCE), and every typed view function refuses a handle whose published kind does not match before it dereferences anything. Audio and script squads MUST add their enum values here — this file is a guaranteed merge collision.
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 3 ("typed derived resources"); §1 ("Domain extensions own meaning inside the module")

gaps.what: NO AUDIO EXISTS ANYWHERE. Grep across src/ include/ tests/ finds zero audio, PCM, wav, mixer or sink symbols. Per owner directive this is built FOR REAL as a resource domain with a headless-but-genuine consumer. COMPLETE, four parts. (1) Resource extension: include/anoptic_res_audio.h + src/resources/audio/res_audio.c — RIFF/WAVE ingest (PCM s16/s24/f32, mono+stereo), strict validation (chunk bounds, sane rate/channels, declared vs actual data length — remote-FS byte truth applies, believe only bytes read), conditioning to one interleaved f32 (or s16) PCM block with an offset-shaped header exactly like `scene_hdr`, adopted as a DERIVED resource keyed "<source>#pcm" with the encoded source rid as its disclosed dependency; served as a counted view `anoresaudio_pcm(read, h)` -> {samples, frame_count, channels, sample_rate}. Write the WAV parser ourselves (~200 lines, no new dep; cgltf/stb/jsmn precedent is single-header vendored, but a WAV reader does not warrant even that). (2) res_kind gains RES_KIND_AUDIO_ENCODED + RES_KIND_AUDIO_PCM; kind_from_path learns .wav. (3) Headless sink: include/anoptic_audio.h + src/audio/audio_null.c — `ano_audio_sink_open/submit_clip/mix_frame/close` pulling clip views BY HANDLE inside a read scope and mixing into a null device, with a byte oracle (running FNV-1a over every emitted output frame) so a test can assert the mixed output byte-for-byte against an independently computed reference. NOT a full audio engine: no device backend, no spatialization. (4) A real consumer: the level file's `audio` array declares ambient clips, the level domain owns their PCM, and level retirement winks the PCM out with the domain — that is the lifetime evidence Stage B needs. CMake: src/resources/CMakeLists.txt gains audio/res_audio.c; new src/audio/CMakeLists.txt + add_subdirectory in root CMakeLists.txt (~line 168); tests/anotest_resaudio.c + registration in tests/CMakeLists.txt (~line 127 pattern).
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Other resource domains" ("Land real ... audio ... resource domains with production consumers"); owner scope decision 1

gaps.what: NO SCRIPTING EXISTS ANYWHERE. Per owner directive this is a REAL resource domain with a tiny expression/bytecode VM as its genuine consumer. COMPLETE, four parts. (1) Resource extension: include/anoptic_res_script.h + src/resources/script/res_script.c — ingest a small expression source (text), tokenize + parse + VALIDATE (arity, unknown identifier refusal, depth bound, no unbounded loop construct), compile to a flat bytecode block (fixed-width ops, constant pool, offset-shaped header), adopt as a DERIVED resource keyed "<source>#bc" with the source rid disclosed as its dependency. Both the source (RES_KIND_SCRIPT_SOURCE) and the bytecode (RES_KIND_SCRIPT_BYTECODE) are registry resources in the naming domain's lifetime. (2) VM: include/anoptic_script.h + src/script/script_vm.c — a stack machine executing the bytecode view by handle inside a read scope, with a fuel/step cap (a hostile script must terminate), a typed result (f64/bool/error), and a small host-binding table so a script can read named engine values. Explicitly NOT a scripting language: no GC, no closures, no user types. (3) res_kind + kind_from_path gain the script kinds and the .anos extension. (4) A real consumer: the level file's `scripts` array declares an `on_load` hook; `ano_reslevel_open` executes it inside the level's domain and its result parameterizes the level (e.g. light intensity, spawn count) — so scripts are load-bearing for what renders, not a test-only toy. Bytecode dies with the level domain. CMake mirrors the audio wiring; tests/anotest_resscript.c asserts parse refusal on hostile sources, fuel-cap termination, and evaluation against an oracle table.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Other resource domains" ("Land real ... script ... resource domains with production consumers"); owner scope decision 1

gaps.what: Save migration is the ONE domain item that is largely delivered — and its gap is precise. Delivered: config v1->v2 (src/config/config.c:170-178, 254-257) with corrupt-config quarantine-and-regenerate; keybindings v1->v2 (src/keybindings/keybindings.c:177-184, 294-297); world save v1->v2 with source-preserving writeback and exhaustive status mapping (src/resources/world/res_world.c:82-95, 179-187); all four exercised in tests/anotest_persistence.c (config v1 migrate :119-123, keybindings v1 migrate :166-172, world v1 migrate :270, unsupported-version refusal :289-297, deep first-valid-generation fallback :185-214, orphan-temp recovery :216-236). MISSING: the world save is a camera pose (anoresworld_state, include/anoptic_res_world.h:17-24) that does not record WHICH LEVEL was loaded, so a save cannot restore a session once levels exist — and main.c:663 loads "autosave" before any level is opened. COMPLETE: anoresworld_state v3 gains the level's logical id (SID or interned name) plus per-level entity state; `ano_resworld_save_load` gains the v2->v3 migration rung (a v2 save with no level id migrates to the default level); main.c opens the level named by the loaded save rather than a hardcoded literal; ANO_RESWORLD_SAVE_VERSION -> 3 and `min_reader_version` set so a v2-era binary refuses a v3 frame rather than rolling the user back. Also fold the three ad-hoc per-client migration ladders into one shared migration grammar so a fourth client (level state) does not hand-roll a fifth.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Other resource domains" ("save-migration resource domains with production consumers"); A.2 "Durable writes and saves"

gaps.what: No production path ever retires a lifetime domain except SAVE_CONFIG at shutdown (src/engine/main.c:685,696,717). Nothing loads a level, unloads it, and loads another — so the wink-out that Model C/E exist to win has never run against real assets, and `ano_res_stats().live_bytes` residual after a level cycle is unmeasured on real data. A.2 is explicit: "retirement must exercise the winning production hierarchy". COMPLETE: an engine-level load/unload/reload driver (a keybound action or a headless test harness) that opens level A, renders, closes it, opens level B, and asserts (a) every level-A handle now resolves to the sentinel, (b) the renderer released its GPU objects before the domain died, (c) `live_bytes`/`live_blocks` return to the pre-open baseline, (d) `retired_pending` drains to zero and `stalled_readers` does not grow, (e) `domains_live` returns to baseline. tests/anotest_resgroups.c already proves domain open/retire/reopen against synthetic rows (:69-102) — this is the same oracle against the real Sponza-scale corpus with the renderer attached.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Lifetime hierarchy"; A.2 "Wink-out"; A.5 exit bar

gaps.what: Config is a flat named-field C struct (`ano_config {float camera_move_speed; float camera_look_sensitivity; bool menu_at_start;}`, include/anoptic_config.h:19-23) with a hand-written jsmn parser per field (src/config/config.c:90-128) — every new setting means editing the struct, the parser, the validator and the serializer. Keybindings already does it right: SID-keyed entries (`anostr_sid action`, include/anoptic_keybindings.h:52-56) with a name<->SID action table. Config also never declares RES_KIND_CONFIG (see typed-kinds) and loads its bytes as anonymous BYTES. Lower priority than the level/audio/script work but it is a genuine spec delta: §2 wants "configs/keybindings persist safely" behind the extension grammar, and the plan's naming rule wants every naming site to be an ANOSTR_SID or interned string. COMPLETE: a SID-keyed typed setting store (key SID, type tag, value union, validator/clamp per key) with one generic parser/serializer, keeping the existing quarantine-and-regenerate and v1->v2 migration behavior byte-identical.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Other resource domains" (config/keybinding domains); §2 ("All asset meaning lives in resource extensions")

gaps.what: Hard-coded registry bounds were sized before any of this existed and the session that set them recorded that no sizing evidence was collected: RES_SLOT_MAX 4096 rows, RES_DOMAIN_MAX 32 domains, RES_READER_MAX 64 reader lanes, RES_POOL_MAX 1 MiB (src/resources/resources_registry.c:23-28). Once a level declares its full closure — Sponza alone contributes ~70 images plus its scene and its "#rgba" derived siblings, on top of shaders, fonts, audio clips and script bytecode — plus a shared-immutable domain and overlapping level domains during a crossfade, the 4096-row and 32-domain ceilings become real. `row_bind` refuses past RES_SLOT_MAX (:369) and `ano_res_domain_open` refuses when no domain slot is free (:634-635); both refusals are silent to the caller beyond a sentinel. COMPLETE: instrument peak rows/domains across a full level-cycle run, record the numbers in docs/benchmarks/, and either justify the constants against that evidence or raise them (the permanent directory `g_directory[RES_SLOT_MAX]` is a static array of atomic pointers — growing it is a publication-scheme change, not a constant bump, so decide this BEFORE Stage C freezes the directory).
gaps.spec_ref: C.4 ("a bounded preallocated directory justified by population evidence"); A.5 exit bar

integration_points: src/resources/resources_registry.c :: ano_res_get (line 756) — the single-copy hit path at :775-779 is where cross-lifetime policy must land; EVERY squad that loads anything touches this function. Highest-collision site in the area.

src/resources/resources_registry.c :: kind_from_path (line 744) — audio (.wav), script (.anos), level (.anolevel) and config (.json) squads all add extensions here. Guaranteed merge conflict; agree the table up front.

src/resources/resources_internal.h :: res_kind enum (line 39) — audio adds AUDIO_ENCODED/AUDIO_PCM, script adds SCRIPT_SOURCE/SCRIPT_BYTECODE, level uses WORLD. Append only; never renumber (res_pub.kind is published).

src/resources/resources_internal.h :: res_registry_adopt(logical, block, deps, dep_count) (line 133) — the ONE ingest door for every extension. Level/audio/script all adopt through it and all pass their disclosed deps through the `deps` argument. Its contract must not change under anyone.

src/resources/resources_registry.c :: res_disposition_allowed (line 255) — currently a counter-bumping predicate; xlifetime-ops turns it into the policy oracle behind real promote/duplicate/share. Anyone calling it today (only tests/anotest_resownership.c:185-195) will see behavior change.

src/resources/resources_registry.c :: res_pub struct (line 34) + publish_locked (line 491) — adding a readable kind/dependency accessor changes what readers may rely on; this struct is also what Stage C's lock-free publication will freeze. Coordinate before widening it.

include/anoptic_resources.h :: ano_res_lifetime_kind enum (line 40) — SHARED_IMMUTABLE must be appended AFTER ANO_RES_LIFETIME_TOOL_IMPORT or the bound check in ano_res_domain_open (resources_registry.c:601-602) silently rejects it.

src/resources/resources_registry.c :: owner_thread() gate (line 110) — ano_res_get, ano_res_domain_open, res_registry_adopt, ano_res_release, ano_res_unload, ano_res_collect are ALL init-thread-only. Audio decode, script compile and level ingest must run on the owner thread in Stage A; no squad may spawn a loader thread.
src/resources/CMakeLists.txt :: target_sources(anoptic_core PRIVATE ...) — audio/res_audio.c, script/res_script.c and world/res_level.c all append here. Three-way conflict.
CMakeLists.txt :: add_subdirectory block (lines 159-172) — new src/audio/ and src/script/ modules register here; asset staging for levels/audio/scripts goes near line 290.

src/render/gltf/ano_GltfParser.c :: parseGltf(VulkanContext*, const char *logical) (line 86) — re-signatured by level-consumer-render to take a lifetime token + a resident anores_t. src/vulkan_backend/render_api.c:143-156 is its only caller.

src/engine/main.c :: main() bootstrap (lines 628-668) and spawn_scene() (lines 117-184) — level open must slot in after ano_res_init and before initVulkan; spawn_scene is deleted and rebuilt from the level view. The save-load at :663 must move after the level is known.
tests/CMakeLists.txt :: lines 127-201 — the add_executable/target_include_directories(src/resources)/add_test pattern that anotest_reslevel, anotest_resaudio and anotest_resscript all copy.
risks: The cross-lifetime aliasing bug (xlifetime-silent-alias) is latent ONLY because production loads everything into the immortal ENGINE domain. The instant any squad opens a real WORLD_LEVEL domain in production — which level-open-close does on purpose — it becomes a live use-after-retire: a second level's handles point into the first level's mi_heap, and ano_res_domain_retire calls mi_heap_destroy on it. Fix this FIRST or the level squad will ship a crash.

DUPLICATE has an unresolved schema collision: the registry is strictly one row per rid (row_bind, resources_registry.c:358) and rid is fnv1a64 of the logical path. Two domains cannot own two copies of "models/x.png" without either a synthetic per-domain logical key or a per-domain payload slot in res_row. This decision gates xlifetime-ops and must be made by one person before three squads write against three different assumptions.

Audio and script are being built with no existing subsystem to attach to — the temptation is a test-only toy. The owner's bar is explicit: PCM must land in a manager domain and feed a sink with byte oracles; scripts must load+validate+execute and parameterize something real. If the level's on_load script and ambient audio do not affect what actually renders/plays, the domain is scaffolding wearing a domain's name (Completion law §0: "A reduced proxy is scaffolding and must be named as scaffolding").

Everything in Stage A is owner-thread-synchronous. Level open will therefore serialize glTF ingest + image decode + WAV decode + script compile for the whole level on the init thread — a multi-second hitch on Sponza. That is CORRECT for Stage A (A.4 bullet 4: finish the synchronous reference versions first) and MUST NOT be 'fixed' by sneaking a loader thread in ahead of Stage C. Record the cold-open wall time as the baseline the ticket system will later have to beat.

res_pub.dependency_count is a uint32 in a struct that Stage C will freeze as the lock-free publication descriptor (C.4). Widening res_pub to carry a dependency POINTER (rather than the count plus a row-side lookup) would put mutable registry state on the read path and violate C.4's 'reader never touches the mutable hash map'. Keep dependency readback owner-side or copy-out; do not hang a pointer into row storage off res_pub.

The renderer copies conditioned scenes into legacy calloc'd ModelAsset blueprints and then unloads the manager handle (ano_GltfParser.c:120-131), whereas fonts retain their handle and read scope. Level-owned assets CANNOT follow the model pattern — if the renderer copies out and unloads, the level domain owns nothing and wink-out proves nothing. level-consumer-render must convert models to the retain-handle pattern, which is a deeper renderer change than it looks.

RES_SLOT_MAX 4096 is a static array of atomic pointers (g_directory, resources_registry.c:87). Sponza's ~70 images x2 (encoded + derived rgba) plus scene blocks plus audio plus scripts plus a second overlapping level domain could approach it faster than expected, and growing it after Stage C freezes the publication directory is a redesign, not a constant bump.

docs/resourcemanager-comprehensive-report.md says orphan-temp save recovery is unimplemented and 7 assertions fail. That is STALE against the current working tree: recover_all_temps exists (resources_registry.c:1333-1360) and tests/anotest_persistence.c:216-236 exercises it. Do not re-implement it. Re-run build.bat 5 to get the true red/green baseline before anyone claims a regression.

gaps.what: The graphics domain has NO skinning, skeleton, or animation concept at all. include/anoptic_res_graphics.h defines no skin/joint/animation types; anoresgfx_scene (h:164-174) carries only vertices/indices/prims/meshes/nodes/children/materials/images/roots. anoresgfx_vertex (h:67-71) is 8 packed floats (pos/normal/texcoord) with no JOINTS_0/WEIGHTS_0/TANGENT. In res_graphics.c the counting pass (393-419), block layout (421-448), and conditioning pass (479-544) never read data->skins/skins_count, data->animations/animations_count, cgltf_attribute_type_joints/weights/tangent, or node->skin. scene_hdr (44-51) has no counts or offsets for them. COMPLETE = new anoresgfx_skin{joint_first,joint_count,inverse_bind_first,skeleton_root}, anoresgfx_joint{node,inverse_bind mat4}, anoresgfx_animation{name,channel_first,channel_count,duration}, anoresgfx_anim_channel{node,path(T/R/S/weights),interp,sampler}, anoresgfx_anim_sampler{input_first,output_first,count} + joints/weights on the vertex; all offset-shaped inside the same deterministic block; scene view exposes counted arrays; ingest conditions every glTF animation sampler (STEP/LINEAR/CUBICSPLINE) and every skin's inverse-bind matrices. Note the engine has NO skinned/animated asset today (assets/ = viking_room, GlassHurricaneCandleHolder, sponza) — the oracle must be a handcrafted .gltf written by the test, in the existing anotest_resgfx.c style.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Graphics extension" (Required: skins, skeletons, animations); A.4 bullet 3 ("full graphics conditioning including skinning/animation")

gaps.what: Embedded images are silently dropped. src/resources/graphics/res_graphics.c:553 is the exact site: `if (img->uri == NULL || strncmp(img->uri, "data:", 5) == 0) continue;` — this discards BOTH data: URI images AND GLB bufferView images (for GLB, cgltf leaves image->uri NULL and sets image->buffer_view). anoresgfx_image (anoptic_res_graphics.h:100-108) is path-only and its own comment admits it ("\"\" when the image is not URI-addressed (embedded images are not yet served)"). Consequence: models/viking_room.glb — which IS staged into the namespace by CMakeLists.txt:290-294 — loads geometry but loses every texture with no error. Buffers already work embedded (cgltf base64 through the arena), so this is images only. COMPLETE = anoresgfx_image gains {uint64 bytes_off; uint64 bytes_len; uint32 mime} so encoded bytes for embedded images live INSIDE the scene block (or are adopted as separate typed resources keyed "<source>#img<N>"), ingest copies data->images[i].buffer_view / decodes the data: payload into that region, and ano_resgfx_image accepts an embedded image entry as a decode source, not just an anores_t of a namespace file. tests/anotest_resgfx.c:224-241 currently tests only an embedded BUFFER (tri_embed.gltf), never an embedded image — a new oracle is required for both the data: image and the GLB bufferView image.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Graphics extension" (Required: embedded images); A.4 bullet 3

gaps.what: Decoded pixels are the one asset class still caller-owned, and their accounting is a one-way lie. src/resources/graphics/res_graphics.c:663 calls stbi_load_from_memory, which allocates through this TU's malloc (default mimalloc heap) — NOT through res_owned_alloc, NOT into any lifetime domain, NOT poolable. Line 683 calls res_registry_external_allocation(&plan, pixel_bytes), which (resources_registry.c:242-253) only increments requested_bytes/serving_bytes/allocations/transfers and is NEVER reversed — the caller frees with ano_aligned_free (ano_GltfParser.c:200, tests/anotest_resgfx.c:271 and :310) and the manager is never told, so ano_res_stats over-reports forever. The public contract even states it: anoptic_res_graphics.h:193 "rgba; // caller-owned: free with ano_aligned_free". COMPLETE = decode into a res_owned_alloc block with a RES_KIND_IMAGE_DECODED / RES_DEST_BULK plan in the caller's domain (stbi has no custom-allocator hook, so decode to a scratch/staging block then copy-once into the owned home, and COUNT that copy via res_account_copy), adopt it under a typed derived key, and serve it as anores_t + a counted view under a read scope; keep an explicit ano_res_release destructive-transfer path for the GPU-upload case so the hand-off is a named operation instead of an accidental raw pointer.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Fonts and images" ("decoded pixels are caller-owned mimalloc blocks" -> "define decoded/conditioned placement and consume semantics"); A.4 bullet 5 (every copy/hand-off/retirement visible to allocator statistics)

gaps.what: The renderer copies the conditioned scene into legacy calloc'd ModelAsset trees and then throws the manager handle away. Exact sites in src/render/gltf/ano_GltfParser.c: :120 `calloc(1, sizeof(ModelAsset))`, :126 calloc meshes, :131 calloc primitives, :401 calloc nodes, :411 calloc childIndices, :418 calloc rootNodes, :405-419 memcpy of node names/transforms/children/roots straight out of the scene view, and :423 `ano_res_unload(lifetime, sceneH)` — the handle dies at the end of parseGltf. flatten_node (:432-455) and model_flatten (:457-463) then walk the COPY. src/vulkan_backend/render_api.c:18 holds `static ModelAsset* g_assets[16]` and :27-30 / :163 dereference it; nothing anywhere frees g_assets or any ModelAsset member (grep-verified: zero free sites) — so the whole legacy blueprint tree leaks for the process lifetime, unowned by any domain. COMPLETE = ModelAsset/ModelMesh/ModelNode/ModelPrimitive (ano_GltfParser.h:20-51) are deleted; the renderer retains {anores_t sceneH, ano_res_reader/ano_res_read} for the process (or for the level domain), and flatten walks anoresgfx_scene views directly. Requires gfx-gpu-binding-derived to land first, because the per-primitive geometryPoolIndex/materialIndex produced at upload are the ONLY fields ModelAsset adds over the scene view. WARNING: retaining Sponza's scene block permanently pins a multi-MiB direct-mimalloc allocation (res_graphics.c:458 sets transfer_compatible for >1 MiB, so it bypasses the domain multipool) that today was released after GPU upload — this is a real, measurable footprint regression that must be counted, not hidden.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Graphics extension" ("The renderer copies a conditioned scene into legacy ModelAsset allocations and unloads the scene handle; consumers do not yet retain uniform manager views/handles" -> "transparent or ticketed delivery to the renderer")

gaps.what: There is no manager home for the GPU-side result of ingest. ano_GltfParser.c:146 (`outMesh->primitives[p].geometryPoolIndex = lodBase`) and :241 (`outMesh->primitives[p].materialIndex = matIdx`) write the only two facts ModelAsset holds that the conditioned scene does not, and they go into calloc'd memory. Likewise the per-parse side arrays at :154-157 (needed/srgb/loaded/bindless) and :180 (stagingBuffers) are plain calloc/free (:218, :394-397), invisible to res_owned_alloc and to ano_res_stats. COMPLETE = a new typed derived resource (add RES_KIND_GRAPHICS_BINDING to the res_kind enum at src/resources/resources_internal.h:39-50) holding a counted array of {geometry_pool_index, material_index, bindless_index[]} parallel to the scene's prims[], allocated via res_owned_alloc with RES_ROLE_DERIVED / RES_OP_ADOPT into the same lifetime domain and adopted under "<source>#gpu" with a res_dependency_meta edge back to the "<source>#gfx" scene rid; and the transient per-parse arrays move to a TRANSIENT_IMPORT domain or a monotonic arena so every byte the parse touches is accounted.
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 3 ("typed derived resources, and real renderer/audio/world consume sites"); A.4 bullet 5

gaps.what: "Every supported material/resource class" is not met, even though the PBR extension factors are thorough. Missing, precisely: (1) anoresgfx_texref (anoptic_res_graphics.h:95-98) is only {int32 image; float scale} — it drops tv->texcoord (the UV SET INDEX), so any glTF using TEXCOORD_1 for occlusion/lightmaps binds the wrong UVs, and it drops tv->has_transform/tv->transform (KHR_texture_transform) entirely; texref() at res_graphics.c:220-227 is the site. (2) Samplers are never read: condition_material (res_graphics.c:229-314) never touches texture->sampler, so wrap_s/wrap_t/mag/min filter are lost and the renderer hardcodes one REPEAT/LINEAR sampler (src/vulkan_backend/texture/texture.c:403-435). (3) The vertex layout drops TANGENT (normal mapping has no tangent basis), COLOR_0, and TEXCOORD_1 — res_graphics.c:486-493 only ever looks for position/normal/texcoord. (4) data->cameras and data->lights (KHR_lights_punctual) are never conditioned, so a level's lights cannot come from the file. COMPLETE = texref gains {uint32 uv_set; float transform[6]; uint32 has_transform}, a sampler table lands in the scene block, the vertex gains tangent + color + uv1, and cameras/punctual-lights become counted scene arrays.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Graphics extension" (Required: "every supported material/resource class")

gaps.what: Two alternate asset routes survive. (1) src/render/gltf/scratch_process.c is a whole orphaned legacy glTF processor (processGltfMeshes over GltfElements/GltfAccessor/GltfPrimitive, raw malloc/free at its lines 16/28/38-39, and a reference to an undeclared `state`). It is NOT in src/render/CMakeLists.txt (which lists only gltf/ano_GltfParser.c), so it does not even compile — it is dead code that still reads as a second parser. DELETE the file. (2) src/render/gltf/ano_GltfParser.h:60 `ModelAsset* parseGltf(VulkanContext*, const char* logical)` is a by-NAME asset route: the renderer hands it a string and gets back a non-manager blueprint. Even though it internally rides ano_res_get, it is the "alternate asset-loading route" the spec forbids, and it is what render_api.c:143/147/152 calls. COMPLETE = scratch_process.c deleted; parseGltf's signature becomes handle-in/handle-out (take an already-loaded anores_t scene + a read scope, return an anores_t binding handle), so no renderer entry point takes an asset name at all.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Graphics extension" ("No path-based parser may remain as an alternate asset-loading route"); A.4 bullet 6

gaps.what: The legacy path-opening font API is still public and still calls FreeType's filesystem seam. include/anoptic_text.h:39 declares `AnoFontId ano_text_font_load(anostr_t path)` and :42 the `ano_text_font_load_lit(pathlit)` convenience macro; src/text/text.c:129-179 implements it, with `FT_New_Face(g_ftLibrary, cpath, 0, &face)` at src/text/text.c:156 — the only FT_New_Face in the tree. Production has ALREADY migrated off it (src/vulkan_backend/text_raster.c:588, :603, :608 all use ano_text_font_load_memory over ano_res_bytes), so the ONLY surviving callers are tests: tests/anotest_text.c:895 (ano_text_font_load_lit on a nonexistent path), :897 (empty-path refusal), :898 (FONT_PATH), :900 (RUNE_FONT_PATH). COMPLETE = ano_text_font_load and ano_text_font_load_lit are deleted from anoptic_text.h and text.c, FT_New_Face disappears from the tree entirely, and anotest_text.c is migrated to ano_res_init + ano_res_get + ano_res_bytes + ano_text_font_load_memory (its 'nonexistent font' and 'empty blob' refusal cases become sentinel-handle and empty-view cases). Strict quarantine (an #ifdef ANO_TEXT_TEST_ONLY private header in src/text/) is the fallback only if the test squad refuses the migration; removal is the spec's first choice.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Fonts and images" ("the public path-based ano_text_font_load/FT_New_Face route remains" -> "Remove or strictly quarantine path-opening asset APIs"); A.4 bullet 6 ("including legacy path-based font loading")

gaps.what: Fonts are manager BLOBS but have no resource EXTENSION: nothing parses, validates, conditions, or types a font. RES_KIND_FONT exists (src/resources/resources_internal.h:48) and is constructed by nothing. Today src/vulkan_backend/text_raster.c hardcodes three logical paths at :28-30 (ANO_TEXT_FONT_LOGICAL / _RUNE_ / _GREEK_), ano_res_get's them at :586, :604, :609, hands the raw bytes to FreeType, and keeps a process-long read scope (structs.h:583-584 textResourceReader/textResourceRead) purely to stop the borrowed blob from moving under FT_New_Memory_Face. The BAKE (AnoFontBake, built by ano_text_font_bake_ranges at text_raster.c:624) lands on a private mi_heap (state->textHeap, :584) — a conditioned, derived, immutable artifact living entirely outside the manager. COMPLETE = a font extension that ingests a font resource, validates it is a scalable outline face, and adopts the glyph bake as a typed derived resource ("<font>#bake") in a lifetime domain, served by handle; the renderer retains that handle instead of owning textHeap; the three logical paths come from config/level data, not #defines in the renderer.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Fonts and images" ("put font/image meaning behind the appropriate resource extension")

gaps.what: The demo scene is three string literals owned by the renderer. src/vulkan_backend/render_api.c:143 "models/viking_room.gltf", :147 "models/GlassHurricaneCandleHolder.gltf", :152 "models/sponza/2.0/Sponza/glTF/Sponza.gltf" — inside ano_render_load_scene_assets (:136-167), which src/vulkan_backend/vulkanMaster.c:595 calls at init. The resulting POSITIONAL asset_id namespace leaks into the public API (include/anoptic_render.h:85-90 anoRenderAssetCount/anoRenderAssetPrimitives) and into game logic (src/engine/main.c:122 asset 0, :128-129 asset 1, :135 asset 2), which spawns by bare integer. COMPLETE = the renderer names no asset; a level/scene manifest resource (world extension) lists model logical paths as SIDs, the engine opens a WORLD_LEVEL lifetime domain, ingests those models through the graphics extension, and the renderer receives handles. anoRenderAssetPrimitives' integer asset_id must become a manager handle or an interned SID. This is the graphics half of a shared deliverable — the manifest FORMAT belongs to the world squad (include/anoptic_res_world.h has only save state today, no level/asset list).
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Other resource domains" ("The demo models remain hardcoded string literals in renderer startup" -> "Eliminate hardcoded demo asset ownership from renderer code"); A.4 bullet 3

gaps.what: The scene block is bake-SHAPED but there is no bake. res_graphics.c:36-51 lays out scene_hdr with 16-aligned uint64 offsets and its own comment says "load-in-place shaped, for the step-7 bake"; :466 zero-fills before writing so output is byte-deterministic. But nothing ever writes that block to disk and nothing ever loads one back: ano_resgfx_scene (:601-649) carefully bounds-checks a block it can only ever have received from ano_resgfx_model in the same process (its own comment at :613-615 anticipates "bake files will come from disk"). COMPLETE = ano_resgfx_bake(read, scene) -> durable framed blob through ano_res_write; a load-in-place ingest that recognizes a baked block and skips cgltf entirely (zero runtime parse); and a test oracle asserting (a) parse(x.gltf) block bytes == bake(x.gltf) bytes, and (b) every view field from the parsed scene equals the view from the baked scene. The SCENE_ARR_OK bounds gate (:616-628) is already the hostile-pack defense and must remain the load-in-place entry check.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Graphics extension" (Required: "conditioned/baked equivalence"); A.4 bullet 4 ("synchronous reference versions of ... deterministic baking")

gaps.what: Derived resources share the file namespace with no type check — a latent type-confusion hole. res_graphics.c:347-352 builds `key = "<srcname>#gfx"` and :353/:592 use res_registry_find/res_registry_adopt on it. '#' is a LEGAL logical-path byte (src/resources/resources_core.c:107-109 byte_ok rejects only <0x20, 0x7f, '\\', ':'), so a caller can ano_res_get(lifetime, "models/viking_room.gltf#gfx"), hit the same registry map slot, and receive the conditioned SCENE BLOCK through ano_res_bytes as if it were raw file bytes — no kind is recorded on the row and ano_res_bytes performs no kind check. The reverse also holds: a real file literally named "x.gltf#gfx" would collide with the derived key. COMPLETE = the registry row records its res_kind (adopt already takes a res_place_plan carrying .kind), derived keys are constructed through one internal helper, ano_res_get refuses to resolve a derived key from the filesystem, and a typed accessor (ano_resgfx_scene etc.) refuses a kind-mismatched handle instead of reinterpreting bytes. This is the "typed derived resources" line item.
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 3 ("dependency disclosure, typed derived resources")

gaps.what: Graphics already RECORDS dependencies but nobody can read them and nothing prefetches. res_graphics.c:572-591 builds a res_dependency_meta array (the source .gltf rid as RES_KIND_BYTES, plus every image path rid as RES_KIND_IMAGE_ENCODED with the srgb bit in .flags) and res_registry_adopt stores it (resources_registry.c:964-974, into row->dependency_storage / row->dependency_count). There is NO public query: include/anoptic_resources.h exposes no dependency or prefetch call, and the renderer instead re-derives the same set by hand — ano_GltfParser.c:159-177 walks SLOT_RULES to recompute `needed[]`/`srgb[]` per image, then ano_res_get's each one at :186. The manager knows the answer and the consumer asks the question again. COMPLETE = a public dependency-disclosure surface (count + counted view of {rid, kind, flags} for a handle) and a prefetch entry that resolves a scene's declared image dependencies into the same lifetime domain; the renderer's hand-rolled needed/srgb recomputation collapses into a gate over the declared list.
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 3 ("resource-declared dependencies/prefetch", "dependency disclosure")

gaps.what: tests/anotest_resgfx.c (365 lines) is the only graphics oracle and covers none of the required-complete state. It has: handcrafted tri.gltf ground truth (:125-182), a data: URI BUFFER variant (:224-241, NOT an image), PNG decode vs a stb_image_write oracle (:244-282), and an optional real-asset smoke (:284-318). It has NO skin/skeleton/animation assertions, NO GLB case, NO embedded-IMAGE case, NO bake-equivalence case, NO manager-owned-pixel-handle case, NO uv-set/sampler/tangent case. The repo also has no skinned or animated asset at all (assets/ = viking_room{.gltf,.glb,.obj}, GlassHurricaneCandleHolder, sponza/) — but assets/viking_room.glb IS already staged into the namespace as models/viking_room.glb (CMakeLists.txt:290-294) and is a ready-made GLB-with-embedded-images case that nothing loads. COMPLETE = handcrafted skinned+animated .gltf written by the test (matching the file's existing self-staging style), a handcrafted GLB (12-byte header + JSON chunk + BIN chunk) with a bufferView image, a data: URI image, a bake round-trip byte-equality oracle, and a decoded-image handle/retirement oracle asserting ano_res_stats accounting balances to zero after unload.
gaps.spec_ref: A.5 Stage A exit bar ("the synchronous reference path passes its complete correctness suite"); A.2 "Verification evidence" ("add missing end-to-end oracles")

integration_points: src/resources/resources_internal.h:39-50 — the res_kind enum. Graphics must add RES_KIND_GRAPHICS_BINDING (per-prim GPU indices), RES_KIND_ANIMATION, RES_KIND_FONT_BAKE. This file is the neutral placement vocabulary the ALLOCATOR/REGISTRY squad owns; every squad will want to extend the same four enums (res_kind/res_role/res_operation/res_destination). Coordinate one edit, not four.

src/resources/resources_registry.c:186 res_owned_alloc — gated on owner_thread() (line 188). Every graphics allocation (scene block, decoded pixels, binding table, font bake) goes through it, so graphics ingest is pinned to the init thread. Any squad adding threaded/async decode collides here.

src/resources/resources_registry.c:242-253 res_registry_external_allocation — accounting-only, never reversed. Graphics is its ONLY caller (res_graphics.c:683). If the registry squad deletes or re-signatures it, graphics breaks; if graphics kills the external-transfer decode path (gfx-decoded-pixels-manager-owned), this function should be deleted with it.

src/resources/resources_registry.c:939-981 res_registry_adopt(key, block, deps, dep_count) — the single entry by which graphics publishes conditioned objects. Typed derived keys (gfx-derived-keys-untyped) and dependency disclosure (gfx-dependency-disclosure) both re-signature it.

include/anoptic_resources.h — needs NEW public surface that does not exist today: dependency query/prefetch, and typed derived-resource accessors. Graphics, audio, script, and world all need it; it must be designed once.

include/anoptic_render.h:85-90 anoRenderAssetCount/anoRenderAssetPrimitives + AnoRenderableDesc — the positional asset_id index space. Killing the hardcoded demo names re-signatures this PUBLIC header, and src/engine/main.c:122/128-129/135 (spawn_asset by integer 0/1/2) is the consumer. ENGINE squad collides.

include/anoptic_res_world.h — must grow a level/scene manifest (model logical paths as SIDs) for gfx-demo-names-renderer-owned to land. WORLD squad owns the format; graphics owns the ingest of what it names.

src/vulkan_backend/vertex/vertex.h Vertex{Vector3 position; Vector3 normal; Vector2 texCoord} — adding tangents/joints/weights/color breaks the static_asserts at src/render/gltf/ano_GltfParser.c:23-34 AND every Vulkan vertex-input description and shader that consumes them. Widest blast radius in this ledger; RENDERER squad must sign off.
include/anoptic_text.h:39,42,48 + src/text/text.c:129-179 — removing ano_text_font_load/_lit breaks tests/anotest_text.c:895-900. TEXT/TEST squads collide.
src/vulkan_backend/text_raster.c:28-30 (ANO_TEXT_*_LOGICAL #defines), :586/:604/:609 (ano_res_get), :624 (ano_text_font_bake_ranges into state->textHeap) — the font-resource-extension seam.
