src/render/gltf/ano_GltfParser.c:86 parseGltf and :457 model_flatten — both re-signature under gfx-renderer-retains-handles; src/vulkan_backend/render_api.c:29 and :163 are the call sites.
risks: Vertex-layout change is the single most dangerous item. Adding TANGENT/JOINTS/WEIGHTS/COLOR to anoresgfx_vertex forces src/vulkan_backend/vertex/vertex.h's Vertex to change, which invalidates the static_asserts at ano_GltfParser.c:23-34, every VkVertexInputAttributeDescription, and every shader in resources/shaders. Sequence skins/animations AFTER the renderer retains handles, or the two changes will fight in the same files.

Retaining scene handles regresses memory footprint measurably. res_graphics.c:458 sets transfer_compatible = (total+1 > 1 MiB), so Sponza's conditioned block is a direct mi_malloc that bypasses the domain multipool. Today ano_GltfParser.c:423 unloads it right after GPU upload; retaining it (required by A.2) pins it for the process. Measure and report this — it is exactly the kind of placement wound Stage B's contest exists to judge, and hiding it makes the contest synthetic.

ano_res_stats is ALREADY lying about graphics bytes. res_registry_external_allocation (resources_registry.c:242-253) adds decoded-pixel bytes to requested_bytes/serving_bytes/allocations/transfers and there is no reverse path — the caller's ano_aligned_free (ano_GltfParser.c:200) is invisible. Any benchmark that reads these counters today reads a number that only goes up. Fix this BEFORE anyone quotes a graphics allocation figure.

Derived keys are unvalidated type punning. '#' is a legal path byte (resources_core.c:107-109), so "models/x.gltf#gfx" is simultaneously a valid filesystem logical path and the registry key for the conditioned scene. ano_res_bytes performs no kind check, so a scene block can be served as raw bytes. This is a live correctness hole, not just a tidiness issue.

GLB currently half-works and fails SILENTLY. cgltf_parse accepts the GLB container and cgltf_load_buffers points buffer[0].data into the borrowed src bytes, so geometry loads — but res_graphics.c:553 drops every bufferView image with no error and no warn. models/viking_room.glb is already staged and would render untextured with a clean log. Whoever fixes embedded images must add the loud failure first.

Deleting ano_text_font_load breaks a 900+ line test (anotest_text.c) that is not owned by this squad. Land the anotest_text.c migration to ano_res_get + ano_text_font_load_memory in the SAME change, or the tree goes red for the text squad.

src/render/gltf/scratch_process.c does not compile and is not in src/render/CMakeLists.txt. Do not 'fix' it — delete it. Anyone who tries to revive it as a reference will waste a day on a dead API (GltfElements/GltfAccessor no longer exist).

The bake format must not be frozen before skins/animations and embedded images land, or scene_hdr (res_graphics.c:44-51) churns and every baked artifact is invalidated. gfx-bake-equivalence is correctly last in the dependency order; resist the temptation to start it early because the block is already offset-shaped.

gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Multipool use" ("Placement must be owned by the selected full hierarchy, not one global threshold"); A.4 bullet 2 ("neutral internal ownership/placement interface capable of expressing all five complete models without changing public handle semantics"); B.2 ("Each model owns a complete allocator-domain descriptor and registry shard")

gaps.what: A.4 bullet 1 requires the truthful global-pool/scoped-pool scaffold naming to REMAIN in live code, tests, logs and env vars until Stage B. The working tree deleted it prematurely. Verified: `git show HEAD:src/resources/resources_internal.h` has `res_placement_t {RES_PLACEMENT_GLOBAL_POOL=0, RES_PLACEMENT_SCOPED_POOL=1}` + `res_placement(void)` at lines 75-83, and `git show HEAD:src/resources/resources_registry.c:84-86` reads `getenv("ANO_RES_PLACEMENT")` once at init. Neither symbol exists anywhere in the working tree (`git grep ANO_RES_PLACEMENT -- src include tests` returns only docs). Today exactly one strategy ships, unnamed, and the engine logs no placement identity at all. COMPLETE = the seam above is selected by name at `res_registry_init`, the two scaffolds are real vtables (`res_placement_global_pool`, `res_placement_scoped_pool`), the choice is read once from `ANO_RES_PLACEMENT=global|scoped` (default `scoped`), it is logged at INFO on init (`resources: placement scaffold '%s'`), and it is reported in stats so a bench run cannot lie about which strategy produced a number. Stage B adds `ANO_RES_PLACEMENT=model-a..model-e` by registering five more vtables and touching nothing else.
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 1 ("live code, tests, logs, and environment variables use global-pool/scoped-pool scaffold names"); A.2 "Hierarchy scaffold" ("Preserve the truthful scaffold names until Stage B")

gaps.what: Domains DO own a real `mi_heap_t` today (resources_registry.c:566, 617) and the multipool is heap-parented (`ano_mem_parent_heap(d->heap)`, lines 569/621) — the audit report's 'default-heap parents' finding is stale, that was the committed baseline. But three classes of allocation still escape the domain heap, so `mi_heap_destroy` is NOT a wink-out for the memory that matters: (1) EVERY transfer_compatible payload — i.e. every payload over 1 MiB, which is every real scene block and big texture — is `mi_malloc_aligned` on the CALLING THREAD'S DEFAULT HEAP (resources_registry.c:163), not `d->heap`; (2) `res_pub` descriptors are bare `mi_malloc` (line 496, freed at 473/895); (3) `res_retired` nodes are bare `mi_malloc` (line 519, freed at 473). It currently does not leak only because `block_free_locked` retail-frees everything and `finish_domains_locked` refuses to run until `pending_blocks == 0` — i.e. the code pays the full retail walk and then ALSO calls `ano_mem_multipool_destroy` + `mi_heap_destroy` (lines 441-444), double-walking. Consequences: Model C/E's headline claim (retire latency = one `mi_heap_destroy`) cannot be measured, and residual-footprint numbers are wrong because the biggest blocks were never in the heap being measured. COMPLETE = every arena, every control record (`res_pub`, `res_retired`, the retired list, the backing struct itself) and every payload including the transfer class is acquired from `res_backing.root` or a parent chain rooted there; `wink()` is `mi_heap_destroy(b->root)` with NO retail walk, and a retail `free()` path exists only for the pre-retirement single-block case. The transfer class must move to a per-domain `mi_heap_t` sub-heap whose blocks can still be handed out (mimalloc routes `mi_free` home from any thread, so an external consumer freeing a `d->heap` block is legal — but then that block must NOT also be reclaimed by `mi_heap_destroy`, so the transfer arena needs its OWN heap that survives the domain, or the release path must re-home it). State that decision explicitly; it is the crux of B.7's 'transfer-compatible consume allocations'.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Wink-out" ("Each wink-out model must own its root heap on one owner thread and retire the complete domain through the promised heap/domain operation"); A.3 ("group pools have default-heap parents instead of owning heaps"); B.2 ("Raw mi_malloc ... is legal only when it is a declared primitive of that contestant, owned by its root, charged to its reports")

gaps.what: Registry shards are allocated by the ROOT (engine) domain, not the owning domain. `root_plan()` (resources_registry.c:331-342) hardcodes `.lifetime = ano_res_lifetime_engine()` and is used for: the hash table (`grow_slots`, line 312, and init at 572), row chunks (`row_bind`, line 377), name storage (`root_block_alloc_locked(RES_ROLE_NAME, ...)`, line 385), and dependency records (`root_block_alloc(RES_ROLE_DEPENDENCY, ...)` at line 967 in `res_registry_adopt`). So a WORLD_LEVEL domain that loads 5000 resources permanently grows the ENGINE domain by 5000 names + 5000 rows + dep arrays, and retiring the level reclaims none of it. This is precisely the thing §1 forbids ('moving the whole registry into a global default heap and calling payload placement ownership is forbidden'). COMPLETE = split `res_row` (resources_registry.c:46-57) into a PERMANENT identity part (rid, secondary hash, slot, generation, tombstone, owner id, name_len — stays in the small permanent directory, root-owned, non-moving, exactly what §1 calls 'the minimal permanent handle directory') and a per-binding DOMAIN SHARD (name text, `res_dependency_meta` array, placement metadata, payload) allocated through `res_placement.alloc` with `RES_ARENA_METADATA` on the OWNING domain's backing and released at wink-out. The hash table stays root-owned only if the model says so (Model B/C/E will want a per-domain shard index instead; the routing seam must permit both). Note rows are safe to move: readers never touch `res_row` — they resolve through `g_directory[]` + `res_pub` — so only `res_pub` needs pointer stability. But `res_test_row_address` (line 1009) + `tests/anotest_resownership.c:161` assert non-moving rows, so the permanent identity part must stay in the non-moving 64-row chunks.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Registry storage" ("Registry shards must be allocated by the contestant's backing ownership domains"); §1 Final ownership grammar; A.3 ("rows/names/hash tables bypass the hierarchy"); B.1 ("Each model must allocate its registry shards, names, metadata, payloads, derived objects, and teardown records according to its own hierarchy; changing only one payload pointer is not a contestant")

gaps.what: The blocker hiding inside registry-shard-ownership; call it out so nobody ships a dangling pointer. `row_bind` (resources_registry.c:358-398) keeps the name in the ROOT domain ON PURPOSE — its own comment says 'Names live in the root metadata domain so collision diagnostics survive retirement of the payload's lifetime domain.' The rid-collision refusal at lines 362-367 does `memcmp(row->name, logical, len)` on a row whose payload may already be retired. Move the name into the owning domain's shard and that `memcmp` reads freed memory after a domain winks out — an ASan find, or worse, silent acceptance of a colliding rid. COMPLETE = the permanent identity record carries a SECOND independent 64-bit hash (e.g. FNV-1a-64 over the same bytes with a different offset basis, or promote rid to 128-bit) plus `name_len`, and collision refusal compares {rid, rid2, name_len} without touching the name TEXT. The name text then legitimately lives in the domain shard (per A.2 'Registry storage', which lists names as shard data) and dies with it. Diagnostics that want to PRINT the colliding name must do so only while the binding is live. Anything less and either the name stays root-owned (violating A.2) or the collision path is unsound.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Registry storage"; A.4 bullet 8 ("Preserve the strong completed correctness work: ... collision refusal")

A.2 "Allocation path" ("Add allocation-destination planning so each contestant can read/condition directly into its home or into an explicit staging/transfer allocation. Count every copy"); A.2 "Whole-file reads" (byte-truth contract preserved)

gaps.what: 'Count EVERY copy' fails today in six specific places. (1) The `res_read_all` staging buffer in `ano_res_get` and `save_adopt` is a real allocation that never appears in `allocations`/`requested_bytes`/`serving_bytes` — invisible peak memory. (2) `row_bind`'s `memcpy(name.data, logical, len)` (resources_registry.c:387) is an uncounted copy. (3) `grow_slots` (line 322-325) rehashes the whole table on growth — uncounted work, and the old table's serving bytes are the only thing charged. (4) `res_registry_external_allocation` (lines 242-253) adds `requested_bytes`/`serving_bytes`/`allocations`/`transfers` but NOT `live_bytes`/`live_blocks`, and nothing ever decrements it — a decoded 4K RGBA image (64 MiB) is invisible to live/peak/residual forever. Asymmetric accounting: `allocations` will exceed `frees` by exactly the external count. (5) The renderer copies the entire conditioned scene AGAIN into legacy `calloc` blueprints (src/render/gltf/ano_GltfParser.c:120-131, 401-419: `calloc` + `memcpy` of nodes, children, roots) and uploads vertices/indices into GPU staging — every byte of it uncounted. (6) `ano_res_release`'s pooled path (resources_registry.c:874-888) `mi_malloc_aligned`s the copy-out block on the DEFAULT heap and counts `copies`/`duplications`/`transfers` but never counts that allocation. COMPLETE = a single accounting choke point (`res_account_alloc/free/copy/transfer/stage`) that every path in `src/resources/` calls, `live_bytes` symmetric for external transfers (charged on hand-out, credited on `ano_res_release`/consumer return), a `hint_mismatch_copies` counter for the spill case from dest-planning, and the renderer's blueprint copy either eliminated (renderer retains the manager view) or charged as `RES_DISPOSITION_DUPLICATE`. Until then no benchmark claim about copies is admissible.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Allocation path" ("Count every copy; no copy is invisible in a benchmark claim"); A.4 bullet 5 ("Make every load, parse, condition, copy, promotion, hand-off, and retirement visible to allocator statistics")

gaps.what: The pooled path silently violates the requested alignment. `owned_alloc_locked` (resources_registry.c:160-165) passes size only to `ano_mem_multipool_alloc` and DROPS `plan->alignment`; the multipool aligns a block to `min(class stride, 4096)` (anoptic_memory_pools.h:120, pools.c:431). So a 20-byte payload whose plan asks for `ANO_CACHE_LINE` (which `ano_res_get` sets unconditionally at resources_registry.c:797, and `res_graphics` at :457) is served from the 32-byte class at 32-byte alignment. `res_read_all` guarantees `ANO_CACHE_LINE` for staging, so the CURRENT copy accidentally hides nothing — but as soon as dest-planning lands direct landing, a small resource lands at 16- or 32-byte alignment while its consumer was promised 64/128. That is a real SIMD/SoA hazard and it silently poisons any Model D/E stripe claim. COMPLETE = `res_site.alignment` reports what the arena will ACTUALLY deliver; `res_owned_plan` REFUSES a plan whose arena cannot meet `plan->alignment` (or routes it to an arena that can); payload multipools are configured with `min_block = ANO_CACHE_LINE` (a deliberate, measurable internal-fragmentation cost on tiny resources — exactly the Model A wound the contest is supposed to expose) or a dedicated aligned class is added to `ano_mem_multipool_cfg`.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Multipool use" ("Metadata, variable objects, bulk data, consumable transfers, and oversize cases each need the allocator class promised by that model"); B.8 ("alignments through 4096")

A.2 "Allocator roster" ("stripe remains a documented idea ... Implement cache-striped placement where the SoA registry/ticket/payload design consumes it; omit no promised tool"); B.6 Model D; B.7 Model E; §0 ("Cache layout is part of the algorithm ... ANO_CACHE_LINE and ANO_THREAD_LINE")

gaps.what: `ano_mem_pool` (the bounded fixed-size pool, pools.c:529-620) has ZERO production consumers — `git grep ano_mem_pool_ -- src` returns only src/memory/pools.c itself. A.2 "Allocator roster" says every shipped allocator needs a real resource consumer and a home bench; B.6 (Model D) requires 'fixed streaming chunks live in bounded ano_mem_pool instances'. COMPLETE = `RES_ARENA_CHUNK` is a real arena backed by `ano_mem_pool_make(parent, 512*1024, ANO_CACHE_LINE, max_blocks)` per domain, wired to the ranged/chunked read path (which is itself missing — coordinate with the streaming squad), with false-on-empty backpressure surfaced rather than silently growing. `ano_mem_pool_reserve` prewarms it. The 512 KiB constant already exists as `RMOS_CHUNK_MAX` (src/resources/resources_os.h:29) — reuse it, do not invent a second number.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Allocator roster" ("the fixed pool has no production resource consumer ... Give every shipped allocator a real resource consumer and home benchmark"); A.2 "Streaming economy" ("No production 512 KiB chunk pool"); B.6 Model D

gaps.what: `res_kind` (resources_internal.h:39-50) has ten values and no `RES_KIND_COUNT` sentinel. Model B is kind-major: it needs a dense, countable kind axis to index per-kind backing domains, and B.4 explicitly names 'audio' and 'scripts' among the domains it must create. The owner's binding scope decision 1 says audio and script resource domains are built FOR REAL. Neither kind exists. COMPLETE = add `RES_KIND_AUDIO_ENCODED`, `RES_KIND_AUDIO_PCM`, `RES_KIND_SCRIPT_SOURCE`, `RES_KIND_SCRIPT_BYTECODE` (and `RES_KIND_PACK` for the pack squad), add `RES_KIND_COUNT` as the dense bound, and extend `kind_from_path` (resources_registry.c:744-754) to classify their extensions. Also add `RES_ROLE_COUNT`, `RES_OP_COUNT`, `RES_DEST_COUNT` — the stats attribution table (below) indexes on all four axes and cannot be sized without them. This is a small edit that every other squad's kind additions will collide on; land it early and once.
gaps.spec_ref: B.4 Model B ("complete backing domains by resource kind: ... audio, scripts, and any other real class established in Stage A"); B.2 ("Every allocation and free is attributed to a resource kind, lifetime, role, and operation")

gaps.what: B.2 requires per-(kind, lifetime, role, operation) attribution 'so aggregate wins cannot hide one catastrophically weak class', plus parent calls, class hits, oversize hits, internal/external fragmentation, promotion cost, release mode, retire cost, registry probes, and residual footprint. Today `ano_res_allocator_stats` (include/anoptic_resources.h:111-133) is ONE flat struct of 21 counters and `res_account_copy`/`res_account_transfer` (resources_registry.c:220-240) both take a `const res_place_plan *plan` and immediately `(void)plan` — the attribution parameter is literally discarded. `serving_size` (resources_registry.c:137-152) also RE-DERIVES the multipool's class math instead of asking the arena what it charged (and its `n > RES_POOL_MAX` branch is dead: `pooled` already guarantees `bytes <= RES_POOL_MAX`), so serving/fragmentation numbers are a guess that will silently diverge the moment a model retunes `ano_mem_multipool_cfg`. COMPLETE = (i) `res_site.serving` is authoritative and `serving_size` is deleted; (ii) an internal attribution table `ano_res_allocator_stats by_kind[RES_KIND_COUNT] / by_role[RES_ROLE_COUNT] / by_op[RES_OP_COUNT] / by_lifetime[6]` fed from the single accounting choke point, with an internal accessor `ano_res_allocator_stats res_stats_axis(res_stat_axis axis, uint32_t key);` (tests/anotest_resbench.c already includes resources_internal.h, so no public surface change is needed); (iii) new counters: `parent_calls`, `class_hits`, `oversize_hits`, `internal_frag_bytes` (serving - requested), `staging_bytes`, `staging_peak`, `hint_mismatch_copies`, `retire_ns`, `residual_bytes` after each cycle; (iv) `ano_res_stats()` (resources_registry.c:990-1007) sums `chunk_bytes` from each domain's multipool but NOT the domain `mi_heap_t`'s own page footprint — residual-footprint claims are therefore short by every page the heap holds. Add `mi_heap`-level accounting or state the omission.
gaps.spec_ref: B.2 ("Each model reports requested bytes, serving bytes, ... parent calls, class hits, oversize hits, internal/external fragmentation, copies and bytes copied, promotion cost, release mode, retire cost, registry probes ... and residual footprint after every cycle"); A.4 bullet 5

gaps.what: Parse staging is a HIDDEN allocator. `ano_resgfx_model` (res_graphics.c:359-365) creates its own `mi_heap_t scratch LOCALHEAPATTR = mi_heap_new()` and its own `ano_mem_monotonic` over it, entirely outside the domain hierarchy and entirely outside allocator statistics — every glTF buffer byte, every cgltf node, the whole ingest working set, invisible. B.2 forbids exactly this: 'Raw mi_malloc, default-parent allocation, a private scratch heap, or any other escape is legal only when it is a declared primitive of that contestant, owned by its root, charged to its reports, and subjected to its teardown and transfer rules. A hidden allocator cannot absorb a model's predicted wound.' Model E's promised 'transient monotonic staging that winks out after adoption' is precisely this arena, and it must be OWNED, not improvised per-callsite. COMPLETE = `RES_ARENA_STAGING` is a declared arena of the backing (a `ano_mem_monotonic` over a per-ingest sub-heap rooted in the domain), obtained via `res_owned_stage()`, charged to `staging_bytes`/`staging_peak`, and released by an explicit `res_staging_wink()` at ingest end. `res_graphics.c` stops calling `mi_heap_new`/`ano_mem_monotonic_make` directly. Same rule will apply to the audio decode and script compile squads — give them the arena, not a scratch heap.
gaps.spec_ref: B.2 ("A hidden allocator cannot absorb a model's predicted wound"); B.7 Model E ("transient monotonic staging"); A.4 bullet 5

gaps.what: `res_disposition_allowed` (resources_registry.c:255-282) is a policy oracle with NO production caller — `git grep` finds it only in its own definition and in tests/anotest_resownership.c:185-191. `RES_DISPOSITION_PROMOTE` and `_DUPLICATE` increment `promotions`/`duplications` when a TEST asks whether they'd be legal, which means the counters count questions, not events. Meanwhile the ONLY real cross-domain move in the codebase is `ano_res_release`'s pooled copy-out (resources_registry.c:873-890), which charges `duplications` + `transfers` + `copies` for what is really a copy-out-to-default-heap. A.4 bullet 7 requires cross-lifetime sharing to be resolved by an implemented, benchmarked policy, and B.5/B.7 make promotion the named hostile ground for Models C and E. COMPLETE = placement owns a real cross-domain move: `int res_owned_promote(res_owned_block *src, ano_res_lifetime to, res_owned_block *out);` which asks `res_placement.plan` for the destination site, moves zero-copy when both sites are transfer-compatible and share a root, otherwise allocates + copies + charges exactly one `copies`/`bytes_copied`/`promotions`, and a `res_owned_duplicate` for the shared-immutable case. `res_disposition_allowed` becomes a pure predicate that mutates NO counters. Real callers: the level squad (a shared texture referenced by two worlds), the graphics squad (TRANSIENT_IMPORT ingest promoted to WORLD_LEVEL).
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 bullet 7 ("Resolve cross-lifetime sharing without silently adding reference counts. Promotion, duplication, shared immutable domains, or another explicit policy must be implemented and benchmarked"); B.2 ("including explicit cross-lifetime policy"); B.5/B.7 hostile ground

gaps.what: Destructive release is a copy for everything under 1 MiB, and the zero-copy path leaks a placement assumption into the public contract. `ano_res_release` (resources_registry.c:865-890): if `transfer_compatible && !pooled` it hands the raw pointer out (zero copy); otherwise it `mi_malloc_aligned`s a new default-heap block and `memcpy`s. `transfer_compatible` is set by the CALLER as `size + 1 > RES_POOL_MAX` (resources_registry.c:798, res_graphics.c:458) — i.e. the 1 MiB threshold is duplicated at three call sites and is not a placement decision at all. C.7 is explicit: 'It is legal only for a placement class whose allocation can be transferred and reclaimed by the recipient without copying or freeing an interior multipool pointer. The allocator contest must therefore give consumable resources a transfer-compatible home. Hidden pooled copy-out is not destructive zero-copy.' COMPLETE = `transfer_compatible` is REMOVED from `res_place_plan` as a caller-set bool and becomes an OUTPUT of `res_placement.plan` (`res_site.transferable`), derived from the arena the model chose — a Model D/E that gives consumable kinds a transfer arena gets zero-copy at ANY size; a Model A that pools everything pays the copy and the benchmark SEES it. Callers stop computing size thresholds. The 1 MiB `RES_POOL_MAX` (resources_registry.c:28) becomes a per-model `ano_mem_multipool_cfg.max_block`, not a global constant.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Multipool use"; A.3 ("pooled destructive release copies"); C.7 ("Hidden pooled copy-out is not destructive zero-copy"); B.2 ("Each model supports ... destructive/worker consumption")

gaps.what: Decoded image pixels are the one asset class the manager does not own at all. `ano_resgfx_image` (res_graphics.c:663-687) lets stb_image allocate through the TU's malloc (mimalloc default heap), calls `res_registry_external_allocation` to ACCOUNT the bytes, and hands the raw `stbi` buffer to the renderer to `ano_aligned_free`. The block is in no domain, is reclaimed by no wink-out, never appears in `live_bytes` (see copy-ledger #4), and cannot be measured for residual footprint. `RES_DEST_EXTERNAL_TRANSFER` and `RES_ROLE_TRANSFER` are set on the plan and then ignored. COMPLETE = `RES_ARENA_TRANSFER` is a real per-domain arena; the decoder allocates INTO it (stb takes a custom allocator via `STBI_MALLOC`/`STBI_REALLOC`/`STBI_FREE` — set them to route through `res_owned_alloc` with the plan, which is the whole point of a placement seam), the block is registry-owned and served by handle, and the renderer either borrows it under a read scope or takes it by an accounted destructive release. This is the concrete Model-D/E 'GPU hand-off / zero-copy rate across the full size distribution' battlefield (B.6 home ground); with today's code that measurement is impossible.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 "Fonts and images" ("decoded pixels are caller-owned mimalloc blocks ... define decoded/conditioned placement and consume semantics through the contest rather than accidental caller ownership"); B.6 Model D home ground; A.4 bullet 5

integration_points: src/resources/resources_registry.c:154 `owned_alloc_locked` — THE single allocation funnel. Every squad's new resource kind allocates through it. It becomes a thin call into `res_placement.plan` + `.alloc`.
src/resources/resources_registry.c:196 `block_free_locked` — the single free funnel; must route through `res_owned_block.site`, not `bool pooled`.
src/resources/resources_registry.c:137 `serving_size` — to be DELETED; it re-derives the multipool's class math. Anyone reading `res_owned_block.serving` must instead trust `res_site.serving`.

src/resources/resources_registry.c:331 `root_plan` / :344 `root_block_alloc` / :350 `root_block_alloc_locked` — the three functions that force registry shards into the engine domain. All three die or change meaning.
src/resources/resources_registry.c:358 `row_bind` — allocates the name; collision refusal at :362-367 reads `row->name` after retirement. The identity/name hazard lives here.
src/resources/resources_registry.c:307 `grow_slots` — the hash table's home domain.
src/resources/resources_registry.c:434 `finish_domains_locked` — the wink-out. Currently `multipool_destroy` THEN `mi_heap_destroy` (a double walk). Becomes `res_placement.wink`.

src/resources/resources_registry.c:599 `ano_res_domain_open` / :638 `ano_res_domain_retire` — where a `res_backing` is constructed and destroyed. The lifetime squad owns the API; placement owns the body.
src/resources/resources_registry.c:756 `ano_res_get` (:787-807 the staging read + copy) — the load path every squad's consumer calls.
src/resources/resources_registry.c:843 `ano_res_release` (:865-890) — the transfer-vs-copy fork. The consume/ticket squad (Stage C) will build on `res_site.transferable`.
src/resources/resources_registry.c:939 `res_registry_adopt` (:964-975 dep-record alloc) — every extension (graphics, audio, script, world) adopts through it; the dep array's home domain changes.
src/resources/resources_registry.c:990 `ano_res_stats` — every bench and test reads it.

src/resources/resources_internal.h:97 `res_place_plan` and :114 `res_owned_block` — the shared vocabulary. Adding `res_site site;` to the block and removing `bool pooled;` / `bool transfer_compatible;` touches every TU that builds a plan: res_graphics.c:450 and :673, resources_registry.c:792 and :1240.
src/resources/resources_internal.h:39 `res_kind` — audio and script squads must add their kinds HERE, and the `RES_KIND_COUNT` sentinel is needed by the stats table. Land the roster edit once, early.

src/resources/resources_core.c:513 `res_read_all` — signature change to a sink ripples into `ano_res_slurp` (:588), `gfx_slurp` (res_graphics.c:118), `save_probe_file` (resources_registry.c:1220) and `ano_res_get`.

include/anoptic_memory_pools.h + src/memory/pools.c — `ano_mem_stripe` is a new public allocator; the collections/log squads may want the same stripe primitive (C.2 demands one shared implementation, not two copies of the cache algorithm).
include/anoptic_resources.h:111 `ano_res_allocator_stats` — additive only; every test that snapshots stats (anotest_resgroups.c, anotest_resownership.c, anotest_resbench.c) reads it.

tests/anotest_resownership.c:151 calls `res_owned_alloc` directly and :161 asserts non-moving rows via `res_test_row_address`; tests/anotest_resbench.c includes resources_internal.h for the placement vocabulary. Both break on a `res_place_plan` field change.

src/render/gltf/ano_GltfParser.c:120-131,401-419 — the renderer's uncounted `calloc`+`memcpy` blueprint copy of the conditioned scene. Any 'zero-copy' or copy-ledger claim collides with the renderer squad here.

NOT touched, by design: include/anoptic_resources.h handle semantics, src/resources/world/res_world.c, src/config/, src/keybindings/ — verified they reference no placement symbol. If a placement change forces an edit to any of these, the neutral-interface requirement (A.4 bullet 2) has been violated.
risks: The name-in-the-domain-shard hazard is a live use-after-free waiting to happen. `row_bind`'s collision refusal (resources_registry.c:362-367) memcmps a name that would live in a retired domain. Anyone who moves names to the owning domain without first landing the secondary-hash identity record ships a dangling read that ASan will catch only when a domain retires with a colliding rid — i.e. almost never in a test, and in production.

`mi_heap_destroy` is single-thread-owner. Every heap must be created AND destroyed on the owner thread. Today init thread == owner thread (`g_reg.owner_thread`, resources_registry.c:110-113) so it holds. Any Stage-B model that wants per-worker staging heaps must create and destroy them on the worker, and Stage C's owner-thread migration must not move a heap across threads. State this in the model contract or a contestant will 'win' by moving allocation off the owner thread and corrupt mimalloc.

The transfer arena is a genuine contradiction, not a detail: a block that an external consumer frees with `mi_free` must NOT also be reclaimed by `mi_heap_destroy` at wink-out. Either the transfer arena has its own heap that outlives the domain, or `ano_res_release` re-homes the block on the way out. Pick one explicitly; getting it wrong is a double-free that only fires when a domain retires with an outstanding transfer.

EOF-truth vs direct landing is a real tension, not an oversight. `rmos_size_hint` is a HINT by deliberate design (the 9P/SMB remote-FS floor exists because sizes lie), and a multipool class block cannot grow in place. 'Read directly into home' is therefore only sound with a spill path. If a squad implements direct landing by trusting the hint, the remote-FS floor test will fail on WSL's /mnt 9P — which is exactly the platform in the verification gate.

Double-accounting between `ano_mem_multipool.st` (pools.c, per-allocator live/peak) and `g_reg.stats` (registry-level live/peak) is currently avoided only because `ano_res_stats` cherry-picks `chunk_bytes`/`chunk_count` from the pools and `live_bytes` from the registry (resources_registry.c:997-1004). Adding arenas multiplies the surfaces; define ONE authority per counter before adding the second arena or the numbers quietly become fiction.

`ano_res_stats().chunk_bytes` ignores the domain `mi_heap_t`'s own page footprint. Model C/E's marquee claim is residual footprint after a level cycle; measuring it with a counter that cannot see the heap's retained pages will produce a flattering, wrong number. Fix the measurement before running the contest, not after.

The permanent bounds (`RES_SLOT_MAX 4096`, `RES_DOMAIN_MAX 32`, `RES_READER_MAX 64`, `RES_ROW_CHUNK 64`) were chosen without sizing evidence — the session's own retrospective says so. Model B wants a domain per KIND (10+ and growing with audio/script/pack) and Model E wants domains per lifetime x role; 32 domains will bind. Raise the bound or make the domain table growable BEFORE B/E are built, not during.

`res_disposition_allowed` mutates `promotions`/`duplications` counters when a test merely ASKS whether a disposition is legal (resources_registry.c:268-276). Any contest number involving promotion cost is currently polluted by predicate calls. Make it pure before any bench runs.

gaps.what:
Owner decision: vendor zstd behind an OFF-BY-DEFAULT CMake option. option(ANOPTIC_ZSTD "Vendor the zstd bulk codec" OFF) in the root CMakeLists.txt; external/zstd/ added as a submodule and add_subdirectory'd ONLY when ON; res_codec.c guards every zstd call with #ifdef ANOPTIC_ZSTD and res_codec_available(RES_CODEC_ZSTD) returns false when absent.
The refusal is the load-bearing part: mounting a pack whose TOC contains a codec-2 entry in a build without zstd REFUSES THE WHOLE MOUNT at ano_res_mount_pack with one ANO_ERROR naming the codec -- it does not lazily fail at first read, and it does not silently skip the entry. A pack you cannot fully read is a corrupt pack (same doctrine as the TOC checksum). Same refusal for codec id 3 (GDeflate: reserved byte only, never implemented -- real.md §13).

gaps.what:
No anopak exists. Implement the FROZEN format from real.md §9 verbatim, with the three holes it leaves closed additively (it never says where the TOC hash lives, never fixes entry size, and never says what payload_hash covers).

Header, 32 bytes, little-endian, serialized FIELD-WISE with the existing put16/put32/put64 helpers (resources_core.c:729-734) -- never fwrite a struct; padding is not portable and the save frame already sets this precedent:
  [0..7]   magic "ANOPAK\0\1"   (7 ASCII + version byte 0x01)
  [8..11]  u32 entry_count
  [12..15] u32 flags = 0
  [16..23] u64 toc_offset       (8-aligned)
  [24..31] u64 toc_hash         (FNV-1a-64 over entry_count*48 TOC bytes)
TOC entry, exactly 48 bytes, sorted STRICTLY ascending by rid:
  [0..7] u64 rid | [8..15] u64 offset (4096-aligned) | [16..23] u64 size (UNCOMPRESSED)
  [24..31] u64 csize (STORED) | [32] u8 codec | [33] u8 hash_id (1=FNV-1a-64)
  [34..35] u16 entry_flags (bit0 = BAKED_SCENE) | [36..39] u32 pad=0 | [40..47] u64 payload_hash
DECISION to record: payload_hash covers the UNCOMPRESSED bytes. It is then the same oracle the loose path can compute (making packed-vs-loose equivalence one hash) and it catches decoder bugs, which a stored-bytes hash cannot. Cost: verifying it requires decoding -- so payload verification is ALWAYS on in the builder, on by default in Debug, opt-in in Release (ANO_RES_PACK_VERIFY); the TOC hash is ALWAYS verified at mount.
Name table WITHOUT touching the frozen header: store the deterministic newline-joined sorted logical-path list as an ordinary entry under the reserved logical path "__anopak/names". Gives the builder its collision check and the tests their enumeration; the format stays literally frozen.

Runtime: include/anoptic_res_pack.h (public: int ano_res_mount_pack(const char *prefix, ano_fspath pack_file)) + src/resources/pack/res_pack.c (mount, TOC validate, bsearch by rid, entry read+decode).
Mount refusal matrix -- ALL of these refuse the mount, never half-mount: bad magic/version; entry_count > (1<<20); toc_offset + entry_count*48 > file size; toc_hash mismatch; rids not strictly ascending (a sort violation is corruption AND breaks bsearch); any offset not 4096-aligned; any offset+csize > file size; any unknown/unavailable codec; any hash_id != 1.
NO mmap (real.md §13: SIGBUS over 9P/SMB). NO cached file handle: entry reads open a FRESH handle per read (rmos_read_open + rmos_read_at + close), because resources_os.h:12 makes fresh-handle-per-read the remote-FS floor doctrine. A squad WILL try to 'optimize' by caching the pack handle -- that silently breaks the 9P floor and must be rejected until Stage C has evidence.
The TOC array is allocated via root_block_alloc(RES_ROLE_REGISTRY) so pack metadata is charged to the ownership hierarchy, not to a stray mi_malloc (A.2 'Registry storage').
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 'Pack and bake' (full frozen pack format, checked TOC, mount); real.md §9 (frozen anopak TOC) and §7 (a pack is just another mount)

gaps.what:
res_candidates (resources_core.c:230) returns ano_fspath only -- it cannot name a pack entry, so packs cannot enter the namespace walk at all. And the ordering law is NOT automatic: today's table is write-root > mounts newest-first > base (resources_core.c:235-249), so a registered pack mount would SHADOW the loose base directory -- the exact inverse of the required 'loose files keep shadowing packs'.

Two changes in resources_core.c:
1. res_mount gains kind (RES_SRC_DIR | RES_SRC_PACK) and a res_pack*; ano_res_mount_pack registers into the SAME ordered table under the same freeze and the same ANO_RES_MAX_MOUNTS cap.
2. New res_candidates_ex emitting a typed source:
     typedef struct res_source { res_source_kind kind; ano_fspath path; const res_pack *pack; uint32_t entry; } res_source;
     int res_candidates_ex(const char *logical, size_t len, res_source *out, int cap);
   THE LAW, enforced structurally by a TWO-PASS walk, not by registration order: pass 1 emits every DIRECTORY candidate (write root, dir mounts newest-first, base); pass 2 emits every PACK candidate (pack mounts newest-first). Loose-shadows-pack is then an invariant of the walk. This is the same one mechanism §7 promises for user overrides, saves, mods, and hot reload.
   Keep res_candidates() as the DIR-only filter for ano_res_resolve/ano_res_exists (which are explicitly loose-only escape hatches: 'a packed asset resolves empty', real.md §11).
3. res_source_read_all(const res_source*, ...) -- the one place that dispatches DIR (res_read_all) vs PACK (bsearch + rmos_read_at + decode). ano_res_get's loop (resources_registry.c:782-812) and graphics' gfx_slurp (res_graphics.c:110-125) both migrate onto it. gfx_slurp is the silent trap: it calls res_candidates directly, so a packed .gltf's sibling .bin would fail to resolve while the .gltf itself loaded.
Open-failure vs read-failure semantics are preserved exactly: a candidate that cannot be opened is skipped (try the next root); a candidate that opens and then fails mid-read is a HARD failure that does not fall through to a lower mount. A corrupt loose file therefore still shadows a good packed copy -- correct, and it matches the existing rc==-1 vs rc!=-1 logic at res_graphics.c:121-122.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 'Pack and bake' (loose-file shadowing); A.4 bullet 4; real.md §7 (a pack is just another mount in the same walk; the write root shadows every read)
A.2 'Pack and bake' (deterministic builder); real.md §12 step 7 ('~200-line offline builder wired into install'); B.8 'Pack/bake workload' (deterministic bake)

gaps.what:
The builder must actually run at install, or 'the shipped scene loads with zero runtime parse work' is prose. Today CMakeLists.txt:311-323 installs the loose resources tree only (shaders, fonts, textures, assets->models).
Add: a custom target anoptic_pack that runs $<TARGET_FILE:anopak> over the staged ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/resources tree (after the shader/font/model staging POST_BUILD steps) producing ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/resources.anopak, plus install(FILES ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/resources.anopak DESTINATION bin/resources).
The bake (bake-loader-path) runs in the same step: the tool ingests each .gltf and emits the conditioned block as the entry '<logical>#gfx', so the shipped pack carries baked scenes and the installed engine never opens cgltf.

Order does not matter for correctness because loose-over-pack is enforced by the two-pass walk, not by registration order.
STATE THIS OR A SQUAD WILL 'FIX' IT: in a Debug/dev build the source resources/ dir mount shadows the pack by design, so cgltf still runs and the zero-parse counter is nonzero. The zero-runtime-parse bar is measured on the INSTALLED tree only. Do not weaken the shadowing to make a bench go green.

gaps.what:
The conditioned scene block is ALREADY load-in-place shaped (res_graphics.c:37-51: scene_hdr with magic 'RGFX', version 1, nine u64 offsets, arrays at 16-aligned offsets, memset-0 before writing so padding is deterministic) and contains ZERO pointers -- image references are inline char path[MAXPATH]. So the bake is not a new format, it is the same bytes written to disk. But it is NOT safe to load from disk today, and this is the sharpest hole in the area:

ano_resgfx_scene (res_graphics.c:607-650) bounds-checks the ARRAY EXTENTS (SCENE_ARR_OK) but validates NOTHING INSIDE them. prim.material indexes materials[], node.mesh indexes meshes[], node.parent indexes nodes[], node.child_first/child_count index children[], children[i] indexes nodes[], roots[i] indexes nodes[], indices[] index vertices[]. Today those are safe only because cgltf conditioning constructs them. The moment a block arrives from a file -- a corrupt pack, a truncated download, a hostile mod -- every one of them is an out-of-bounds read primitive handed straight to the renderer.

Complete state, SCENE_VERSION -> 2, header grows two u64:
  uint64_t layout_id;   // compile-time FNV over sizeof/alignof of every struct in the block
                        // (vertex, prim, mesh, node, texref, image, material). A baked block is a
                        // POD image of C structs -- struct layout is now an ABI. Mismatch = clean
                        // refusal instead of a silent miscompile-grade misread.
  uint64_t block_hash;  // FNV-1a-64 over the whole block with this field zeroed.
Plus static_asserts freezing sizeof/alignof of each struct (all are alignof<=4: mat4 is float[4][4], everything else is float/char/uint32 -- 16-aligned offsets cover them with room to spare).
Plus ONE validation pass, hoisted to load/adopt time (not per-view -- a view is on the hot path): extents (existing SCENE_ARR_OK), then every cross-reference above, then block_hash. Refuse the whole block on any failure, one log line, sentinel out.
Aliasing note, resolved so nobody re-litigates it: the block is allocated storage with no declared type, so reading it through anoresgfx_vertex* etc. is legal under C23 6.5p7 ('the effective type of the object is simply the type of the lvalue used for the access'), and the 16-aligned offsets over a cache-line-aligned base satisfy alignment. The bare casts at res_graphics.c:630-646 may stand; real.md §9's memcpy/union-punning rule applies to reinterpreting a value in place, not to typed access into untyped allocated storage.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 'Pack and bake' (baked model, runtime fix-up path); real.md §9 'Load-in-place aliasing policy'; A.2 'Graphics extension' (conditioned/baked equivalence)

gaps.what:
Nothing writes or reads a baked scene. ano_resgfx_model (res_graphics.c, adopting under key '<source>#gfx' at line 348) always runs cgltf. Complete state:

1. ano_resgfx_model asks the NAMESPACE FIRST: ano_res_get(lifetime, "<source>#gfx"). If the walk (pack or loose) has it -> gulp + validate (bake-scene-v2's one pass) + adopt into the same domain, same anores_t out, cgltf never touched. Only on absence does it fall back to the cgltf ingest. That is literally 'the res_graphics ingest degenerates to gulp + fix-up into the same allocator, same handle out' (real.md §12 step 7).
2. Builder side: the bake emits exactly the bytes ano_resgfx_model's ingest already produces for that source, as the pack entry '<source>#gfx' with entry_flags bit0 (BAKED_SCENE). Equivalence is therefore a memcmp, not an interpretation.
3. A test-visible parse counter (res_gfx_parse_count) that MUST read 0 after loading a baked scene -- the only honest oracle for 'zero runtime parse work'. Prose is not evidence (Completion law, §0).
Dependency disclosure survives the bake: the deps array res_registry_adopt already receives (res_graphics.c:592) is rebuilt from the baked block's images[] at load, so prefetch/dependency behavior is identical on both paths.

gaps.what:
No hot reload exists. New src/resources/resources_reload.c, dev-gated (Debug or ANO_RES_HOT_RELOAD), synchronous and frame-boundary driven so its correctness does not depend on Stage C.

  // Poll at a frame boundary. Output: number of resources swapped; changed rids via out.
  int ano_res_reload_poll(anores_t *changed, int cap);   // include/anoptic_resources.h

Three-step protocol, and step 2 is the whole point:
1. CANDIDATE (cheap, may lie): for each bound row with provenance NAMESPACE, re-run res_candidates_ex and compare (a) the WINNING SOURCE IDENTITY against the one the row recorded, and (b) rmos_stat_hint's (mtime,size) against the recorded pair. A newly-appeared loose file over a packed entry is a candidate even though the row's path is the pack -- that is 'loose-over-pack hot reload, one mechanism' (real.md §7) and it is the case a squad will miss by only diffing mtime on the recorded path. 500 ms poll budget via ano_timestamp.
2. CONFIRM (authoritative): res_hash_file the candidate and compare to row->content_hash. mtime lies on 9P/SMB (real.md §13 rejects mtime selection outright), so mtime is ONLY a filter; the content hash decides. This also kills the reverse false positive: a touched-but-byte-identical file must NOT reload.
3. PUBLISH: load the new generation OUT OF PLACE into the SAME lifetime domain, validate fully (for a scene: bake-scene-v2's whole pass), then republish_locked. A reader inside an open read scope keeps seeing old-complete bytes; the old block is freed only after its retire epoch is passed (ASan proves no UAF).
Wire one call into the engine frame loop (src/engine/main.c), Debug only.
gaps.spec_ref: A.2 'Pack and bake' (hot reload); A.4 bullet 4 ('hot-reload validation'); C.10 (confirms content rather than trusting mtime); real.md §12 step 7

gaps.what:
Reloading models/x.gltf must invalidate models/x.gltf#gfx, and the graph edge needed to find it does not exist. res_dependency_meta / row->dependency_storage (resources_internal.h:108, resources_registry.c:53) record PARENT -> CHILDREN (a scene declares the images it needs). The reverse edge (source -> derived) is nowhere, so a reload of a source leaves a stale conditioned scene published and serving old geometry forever -- a silent correctness bug that looks like 'hot reload doesn't work' and is actually 'hot reload lies'.
Minimum complete: on a confirmed reload of rid R, scan bound rows for any whose dependency_storage contains R, and cascade -- retire them (consumers re-get and re-ingest) or re-ingest them in place through the same republish path. Bounded scan over 4096 slots at a 500 ms poll is free; a reverse index is a Stage-C optimization, not a Stage-A requirement.
The derived '#gfx' key means the cascade is also how a baked-vs-loose flip propagates: dropping a loose .gltf over a packed '<src>#gfx' must retire the packed scene and re-ingest from the loose source.
gaps.spec_ref: A.4 ('typed derived resources', 'dependency disclosure'); A.2 'Pack and bake' (hot reload); C.10

risks: Windows positional read: ReadFile with an OVERLAPPED offset on an ordinary synchronous handle IS positional and blocking, but per MSDN it also advances the file pointer as a side effect. So rmos_read_at on Win64 is single-owner-per-handle in Stage A. Signature is already Stage-C-shaped; Stage C opens per-worker handles or flips to FILE_FLAG_OVERLAPPED+event. Document it in resources_os.h or someone will assume it is thread-safe and ship a data race.

A short pread over 9P/SMB is NOT EOF. res_read_all is accidentally safe because it only breaks on got==0 (resources_core.c:554). A naive res_read_range that treats got<cap as EOF will silently truncate ranges on the remote-FS floor -- the exact byte-truth violation A.2 forbids. Only *got==0 means EOF.

LZ4_COMPRESSBOUND(512 KiB) is ~514 KiB and does NOT fit a 512 KiB pooled chunk. The codec's uncompressed frame must be 496 KiB (bound ~498 KiB) so the compressed side fits one chunk. Get this wrong and either the pool block silently grows or the encoder overruns.

A baked scene block is a POD image of C structs, so struct layout/padding becomes an ABI the instant a pack ships. Without layout_id + static_asserts on sizeof/alignof, a compiler or struct change between builder and runtime is a silent misread, not an error. (Alignment itself is fine: mat4 is float[4][4], every block struct is alignof<=4, and offsets are 16-aligned over a cache-line-aligned base. Aliasing is fine too: the block is allocated storage with no declared type, so typed access is legal under C23 6.5p7 -- the existing bare casts at res_graphics.c:630-646 may stand.)

ano_resgfx_scene validates array EXTENTS but nothing INSIDE them: prim.material, node.mesh, node.parent, node.child_first/count, children[i], roots[i], indices[i] are all unchecked. Safe today only because cgltf constructs them. The bake turns every one of them into an out-of-bounds read primitive fed straight to the renderer. This is the highest-severity item in the area.

Caching the pack's file handle across reads breaks resources_os.h's fresh-handle-per-read doctrine (the remote-FS floor). It looks like an obvious optimization and will be attempted. Reject it until Stage C has queue-depth evidence.

In a Debug/dev build the source resources/ dir mount shadows the pack BY DESIGN, so cgltf still runs and the zero-parse counter is nonzero. The zero-runtime-parse bar is measured on the INSTALLED tree only. A squad chasing a green bench will 'fix' the shadowing and destroy the loose-over-pack invariant.

4096-aligned payloads waste up to 4095 bytes per entry. Negligible for Sponza (~70 files); it would bloat a corpus of thousands of small audio clips. Keep the alignment (it is the frozen format and keeps the DMA/O_DIRECT door open) and revisit only with evidence.

rid = FNV-1a-64 over the logical path is the pack's bsearch key. A collision breaks the search silently. The builder must hard-fail on a collision naming both paths -- do not rely on the runtime registry's collision refusal, which fires too late.

Ranged reads into an LZ4/zstd-compressed entry are unsupported in Stage A (sequential frames carry no random-access index) and must be stated as an explicit non-goal, or a squad will ship a seek that silently returns frame-boundary-aligned garbage. Already-compressed assets (audio, PNG/JPEG) are stored RAW by builder policy, so the streaming shape is unaffected.

Hot reload's derived-resource cascade depends on an edge that does not exist (dependency metadata records parent->children, never source->derived). Without it, reloading a .gltf leaves the stale conditioned '#gfx' scene published and serving old geometry -- hot reload that appears to work and is lying.
